states.title: Raider
states.description: The Pandemonium doesn't just bring out monsters—thief packs like the Rigland Raiders are known to use the chaos to their advantage.

states.title: Flamby
states.description: A hot-tempered bird commonly found in the plains. Although they usually prey upon insects and small rodents, they have been known to attack passersby when startled.

states.title: Bikki Punk
states.description: Like any region, the Kawazu Wetlands has its share of wayward youths. With the right guidance, however, they have the potential to become loyal allies.

states.title: Salaak
states.description: Salaak are a species of semi-intelligent reptiles. Some sziviik have uprooted them from their homelands, and now keep them as servants and pets.

states.title: Orc Ward
states.description: Orcs choose roles by body shape and size. The most massive are chosen as guardians, who protect chieftains and are sometimes hired out as bodyguards.

states.title: Gob Pilferer
states.description: Goblins are irksome pests that like to mimic human clothing and culture. They're very bad at it. This makes their raids not just dangerous, but insulting.

states.title: Sellsword
states.description: Growing up in a rural village, she trained with the blade for this moment. Now the world's in need of a good sword, and she's ready to make her fortune.

states.title: Urd Warrior
states.description: Urds are a primitive woodland species that has recently learned to craft tools. Elves consider Urds a nuisance, and sometimes set Urd traps around their settlements.

states.title: Souldier
states.description: When soldiers die at sea, the necrotic energies of the deep can bring their fish-eaten corpses back to fight new, unholy wars. For this reason, many cultures avoid naval battles.

states.title: Nascent Hero
states.description: She's out to become the hero of her own story. But since she's not that skilled, she bands with others to learn their fighting techniques.

states.title: Bikki Novice
states.description: Bikki must prove themselves to their clans before coming of age. For many, this means finding honorable battles wherever they can. Thus, they're often on the road.

states.title: Bikki Master
states.description: Bikki swordmasters travel the roads and watch out for their younger siblings, trying to make sure they don't die while trying to prove themselves.

states.title: Magmacarp
states.description: These strange fish are born in magma flows, sometimes creeping out to burn forests and homes and to feed on the ash left behind. Captured and fed, they can fuel a stove indefinitely.

states.title: Fire Brigand
states.description: Remnants of the dead Fire Clan still roam the countryside. Once, they used their skills to uphold a syndicate. Now, they are reserved for basic survival.

states.title: Serpent Idol
states.description: For some strange reason, statues of serpents come to life during the Pandemonium. Every single one. Some wonder why people continue to build serpent statues.

states.title: Aetherfoal
states.description: Elvish Aether Knights capture these ephemeral horses early in their training. An Aether Knight without her mount is not worthy of her coat of arms.

states.title: Aethersteed
states.description: Wild Aethersteeds storm through the sky before major natural disasters. The phrase, "the Aethersteeds march" expresses fear for terrible things to come.

states.title: Gargancrab
states.description: These strange land crabs grow so quickly that they shed their rocky shells monthly. Some brave farmers cultivate them for building materials, which cuts down on mining.

states.title: Leviacrab
states.description: Evolved Gargancrabs not only shed their rocky carapaces, but also trace amounts of precious jewels. Unfortunately for crab farmers, they're far more vicious than their unevolved relatives.

states.title: Wantonwitch
states.description: Many dream of entering Obscura Academy, one of Westland's most historical magic academies. However many feel their experience at the school does not quite live up to their expectations.

states.title: Wantonwitch
states.description: Obscura dropouts are not rare, and many of them have found inventive ways to use the skills they learned during their student years. While not always on the up and up, no one can deny their creativity and pluck.

states.title: Cinderwolf
states.description: This hot-blooded wolf is infused with the elemental essence of fire. Farmers experimented with domesticating them, but this resulted in torched fields and livestock.

states.title: Blazewolf
states.description: Packs of these wolves have been known to terrorize camping travelers. They don't steal food—they simply sleep in their campfires.

states.title: Bikki Rogue
states.description: Bikki warriors train to take advantage of their unique bodies. They strike and leap out of range in the blink of an eye. They win through a thousand cuts, not one hard swipe.

states.title: Bikki Rake
states.description: Bikki must prove themselves to their tribes. Some prove themselves through battle. These warriors prove themselves by enacting swift and brutal justice.

states.title: Levinwolf
states.description: Levinwolves generally live in mountains, but will come down to flat ground if overpopulation has driven down food supplies. This year, they've multiplied dramatically.

states.title: Levintoline
states.description: Wild Levinwolf packs center on an alpha Levintoline wolf. Alpha levintolines have always mothered at least seven pups. The correlation is unknown.

states.title: Baltu Merc
states.description: Baltu'kud mercenaries fighters fill many for-hire armies worldwide. They're tough, they're fearless, and they're loyal. While not perfect conversationalists, they're perfect soldiers.

states.title: Baltu Merc
states.description: Although exceptional warriors, baltu'kud mercenaries have one major weakness; their voracious appetites. They have been known to abandon wars at pivotal moments because they simply wanted something their commanders refused to give them.

states.title: Sarru Sun
states.description: Each woman of the Sarru must serve five years as a mercenary outside her tribe, giving her wages to her tribe before earning the right to marriage and to lead a household.

states.title: Sarru Moon
states.description: Every Sarru mercenary woman rises through three "circles" of skill. In the second circle, she begins wearing a ceremonial garment denoting her esteemed station.

states.title: Finn
states.description: Finn is a cheerful, friendly young man from the village of Rossé in the Rigland Central Plains. He loves his peaceful life in the countryside, but a powerful calling to help those in need, inspired by his mother's stories of the father he never knew, will lead him on a wild adventure far from the comfort of home.

states.title: Kwibble
states.description: Kwibble burrow into the earth to sleep, but they leave the feelers on their heads poking out above the ground. They respond viciously to people and animals who mistake them for food and attempt to pick them.

states.title: Krish
states.description: Krish are not normally aggressive. However, during the Pandemonium they get particularly defensive of their territories since monsters threaten their dens' safety. Trained Krish are common battle companions for young knights.

states.title: Rizette
states.description: Rizette is a young hunter from Westcarte, a village near Rossé in the Rigland Central Plains. Her unassuming demeanor hides an indomitable spirit and a loyal heart. A tragedy forces Rizette to flee her home with her younger brother Theo, of whom she is fiercely protective.

states.title: Ludmilla
states.description: Openhearted and an excellent leader, Captain Ludmilla commands the Third Regiment of the Rigland Red Dragon Knights. Her rigorous training routines that turn everything, including sleeping, into some form of training, are infamous among the Knights.

states.title: Nagi
states.description: A hopeless romantic with a silver tongue, Nagi is a bikki swordmaster who compares every woman he meets to a variety of flower. Don't mistake him for a simple dreamer though—Nagi is a man of action who fights with breathtaking agility and masterful swordsmanship.

states.title: Astrid
states.description: A proud perfectionist, Astrid is determined to live up to the expectations placed on her as daughter of the Commander of the Red Dragon Knights. She is as strict on others as she is on herself, but her air of confidence hides insecurities that she doesn't have what it takes to follow her father.

states.title: Lightling
states.description: Lightlings are highly sought after by many would-be monster trainers. Their winged adult forms can fly while carrying a passenger, making them excellent companions in battle. Because their sheer ferocity makes them nigh impossible to tame as adults, hunters often try to capture them while they are still infant Lightlings. However, this task is far from easy, as poisonous claws and a nasty bite make even Lightlings quite dangerous.

states.title: Shockling
states.description: While finding and capturing a rare Lightling is a test of skill for a monster trainer, taming the older Shockling is a test of patience. In addition to being much larger, Shocklings are also more aggressive and fearless, requiring constant supervision to keep them out of trouble. However, despite their disobedient tendencies, Shocklings care deeply for their masters, and they will protect them from any threats with zeal. They also like to bring their master fresh prey each morning, though this is not always quite as appreciated.

states.title: Flittle
states.description: The flowers of Flittles contain a pollen that aids sleep. For this reason they are highly sought after by apothecaries, who use honey to distract the Flittle while they harvest the pollen.

states.title: Slizzle
states.description: Slizzles slurm through the slippery slog of slathering slop of the Caves of Reia. Despite their animalistic appearance, they're rather intelligent, and have been found slurping and slathering little messages to one another. They also apparently communicate by intentionally manipulating their strange star patterns.

states.title: Slazzle
states.description: Slazzles slip and slide through the slime and slick of the Caves of Reia. It's said that if one follows the star charts that surround their bodies, it'll lead to a great treasure. This is an odd rumor, since the stars move rapidly and don't seem to correspond to Erden's cosmology.

states.title: Nocteddy
states.description: Nocteddies are woodland bears that sometimes travel into the plains when their food sources are threatened. During the Pandemonium, monsters eat up more than their fair share of foods, so Nocteddies go looking wherever they can find a meal.

states.title: Noctursus
states.description: While not generally known to attack humans, Noctursus will look for food wherever they can get it. On the other hand, this means they will make dedicated, fierce allies if given a reliable food source. Some woodland people even rely on them as hunting companions.

states.title: Tiger Hornet
states.description: Recently introduced to the local ecosystem from faraway lands, Tiger Hornets were pests when normal sized. Now, they destroy crops and menace villages.

states.title: Model S1
states.description: Originally invented to eradicate pests, these self-replicating metal spiders have instead spread beyond their inventor's control. Watch out, they like to hunt in packs.

states.title: Yanti Pike
states.description: After one ill-fated romance with a human prince and a sea witch's curse, the yanti'kud fell victim to one of history's worst propaganda campaigns, painting them as murderous seducers. The stereotype still persists.

states.title: Yanti Pike
states.description: Despite the persistent stereotypes and fairy tales about yanti mating habits, yanti soldiers are stellar fighters, capable on land, but utterly devastating at sea. Many yanti youth are keen set the record straight and bring honor back to the yanti'kud.

states.title: Desertle
states.description: These odd creatures are the desert sand made flesh. Every human culture has a story to explain why these mysterious monsters exist.

states.title: Nomad
states.description: Some eschew the cities, scoffing at their false security. These nomads tend to gather in nomadic bandit tribes that come and go like the wind.

states.title: Sprout
states.description: The boom in magic during the Pandemonium often causes plantlife to awaken as monsters. While weak and erratic, these plants can become aggressive when the Eternal reigns.

states.title: Returner
states.description: Returners are warriors who died in disgrace and are bound to their old armor, given a second chance to redeem themselves or embrace monstrosity.

states.title: Sapph Knight
states.description: Unlike most knights, warriors of the Sapphire Order are renowned for support roles. They foster others' greatness on the battlefield, and share in the glory.

states.title: Air Sentinel
states.description: Air Sentinels come from ancient elven statuary meant to guard ruined temples, animated by the elemental essence of air. They now see all living things as threats to their temples.

states.title: Lutrinox
states.description: Lutri travel in swarms, feeding primarily on birds, such as ignigeons. When the birds migrate in winter, they ravage the land looking for a different kind of prey.

states.title: Topazpion
states.description: Topazpions are the only desert creatures which even the mighty sziviik avoid at all cost. A single snap of its pincers can break rocks, and of course, bones.

states.title: Dinowraith
states.description: Fire spirits like to possess the terrifying lizards of aeons past. Their bones hold up well to the blaze. Were these extinct monsters immune to fire?

states.title: Frozen Soul
states.description: These spirits are usually friendly, but when the Pandemonium throws the energies of the world out of balance they turn vicious.

states.title: Orc Vet
states.description: Orcs denote rank with bone adornments. A skull helm is the sign of an accomplished warrior.

states.title: Holy Gaze
states.description: Holy Gazes are elemental expressions of life, and are known to give strange, prophetic dreams to those sensitive to life energies.

states.title: Mandragora
states.description: It is said that mandragora scream when pulled from the earth and all who hear their voices perish, but they are a valuable potion ingredient for apothecaries.

states.title: Demonkey
states.description: This monster is a vicious earth spirit possessing a giant monkey. It drains the life energy from nearby plants to fuel its remarkable resilience and ferocity.

states.title: Voltstruct
states.description: When severe storms occur in places of intense magical history, sometimes the ground itself becomes animated. Voltstructs are the ground and thunder made sentient.

states.title: Frostfenris
states.description: During the Pandemonium, winter wolves became infused with spirits of cold. This turned the already proficient hunters into ferocious magical predators.

states.title: Levinrachnid
states.description: While deadly, these electricity-infused spiders are hunted for their energy-producing glands, which are used as high-end lighting for city streets and wealthy homes.

states.title: Levinwidow
states.description: A folk parable tells of a farmer seduced to a lake by a mermaid. A Levinwidow electrocuted the mermaid and saved the farmer woman. Its meaning has been lost with time.

states.title: Jynn
states.description: Jynns are mischievous thunder spirits featured in many elvish tales. While they are not immortal, it's often best to simply wait for them to get bored and leave.

states.title: Hauntshroom
states.description: Every Hauntshroom is possessed by a long-dead human or elf. Some mediums specialize in trying to contact clients' ancestors by finding their respective hauntshrooms.

states.title: Needlepest
states.description: Most monster plants are simply annoying. Needlepests, possessed cacti, are downright deadly. They're not very robust, but they're difficult to engage in close combat.

states.title: Szi Pilgrim
states.description: Many szi tribes forage and spread when food runs short. Pilgrims scout for new homelands for their tribes, reporting back once they've found fertile ground.

states.title: Wendigo
states.description: During the Pandemonium, great mountain apes froze to death, later breaking out of their icy shells imbued with cannibal spirits. Now, they feast on any bit of flesh they can find.

states.title: Elven Knight
states.description: The elite knights of Elvenlent have mastered "bone weapons." Not actually made of bone, these ancient elven weapons are built hollow like a bird (and an elf's) bones in order to reduce weight for quicker strikes.

states.title: Orc Shaman
states.description: Orcs maintain strong ties to nature. Each tribe trains a cadre of shamans who travel outside the tribe to understand other cultures. This can be complicated since they barely understand human tongues.

states.title: Erish
states.description: Erish are a strange hybrid of plant life and wildlife. When spiritual energy erupts in a place inhabited by both, sometimes they fuse to create a completely new, stronger creature. Erish are one of the more common examples of this magical phenomenon—a hybrid of a fox and ivy.

states.title: Trissle
states.description: Trissles are very common all over Erden. They're even popular pets in many cities, although this ends up making them something of a pest problem because they're very wild in adulthood and are often abandoned. So trissles are often found raiding urban garbage and demolishing gardens.

states.title: Treasle
states.description: One of the most important children's stories in Erden, the Treasle and the Troubadour, put Treasles forever in the cultural eye. In the story, a troubadour lost her hand in war, and couldn't play the lute. After falling to depression, a whimsical Treasle showed her other ways she could tell her stories to the world.

states.title: Pike Apostle
states.description: The lesser-ranking members of the Pike Paladins are determined to prove themselves to the rest of their order. They will accept any challenge in the hopes of earning the privilege of wielding a spear, the holiest of weapons. Some set out on pilgramges across the land, vanquishing evildoers and dedicating their victories to their idol, the legendary Vensha Dayne.

states.title: Pike Apostle
states.description:
Different factions of the Pike Paladins exist, and they often disagree about certain aspects of Vensha Dayne's lore. They meet regularly to debate and share ideas.

Some believe Vensha defeated an evil aquatic demon by having a yanti witch cast a spell on her so she could breathe underwater. Others believe this battle took place on land, and it was the demon who was able to breathe air by tricking a yanti witch into assisting her.

states.title: Cryosaur
states.description: When the Pandemonium hits, spiritual energy finds its way into numerous objects. Sometimes, this means those objects become animate. And in the Silverdrake Mountains, this sometimes means fossils of long-extinct dinosaurs. Cryosaurs are these fossils, resurrected by the magic of the cold.
Szi knights are trained specifically to clear paths and territories for tribal migration. They police roads along their migrant caravan voyages.

states.title: Szi Paladin
states.description: Szi paladins are renowned for their ability to cut through defenses. They're trained to carve through bandit encampments and nests of vicious monsters with ease.

states.title: High Lancer
states.description: The beliefs of the Pike Paladins, a secret society of spear enthusiasts, are based on the legend of Vensha Dayne. According to their stories, Vensha developed supernatural skill with a lance after lightning struck her family home when she was still a child.

states.title: High Lancer
states.description: The Pike Paladins travel Westland speading the word of their great founder. Only the highest-ranking members of the order are permitted to actually wield spears, however—those who have yet to prove themselves must use other, "lesser" weapons.

states.title: Elf Seaspire
states.description: Eves is a Seaspire. Seaspires are an elite elf anti-terrorist order which operates freelance for the various empires. They exist to stop pirates and seamonsters.

states.title: Elf Seaspire
states.description: Eves developed a mysterious form of armor which operates perfectly underwater. He equipped the entire order in his invention. Now, one Seaspire can sink a pirate cruiser if undetected.

states.title: Elven Starcry
states.description: Alina studies thunder magic, but not to cast spells—she internalizes the power of air to enhance her martial abilities, slashing with thunder and blocking with gale force.

states.title: Elven Starcry
states.description: Alina has mastered thunder energy—when she moves, it's like a dance that cracks with energy. Her voice does not come from herself—it comes from the sky.

states.title: Baltu'Kud Fleshrender
states.description: Takesh fancies himself the pinnacle of baltu'kud warrior. His hobby is being exiled from cities. He collects death sentences like others collect seashells as his barroom brawls become bloodbaths.

states.title: Baltu'Kud Fleshrender
states.description: Takesh once told a bar full of people that he'd only be ready to die once he tasted the blood of the last living thing left alive. When told that if he was the last thing alive, he'd be tasting his own blood, he killed the person who brought it up.

states.title: Elf Crusader
states.description: Yowyn is a paladin from the last Pandemonium, lost in the current world. He perks up any time people speak of the truth of the past event, but he can neither speak nor write about it for some strange reason.

states.title: Elf Crusader
states.description: He fights without a tongue. This is challenging, because elf knights use complex shouts to stop their enemies' nervous systems momentarily. It's said that Yowyn has learned telepathic communication to compensate. However, nobody has heard his telepathy. Despite his ability to communicate, he chooses to remain silent.

states.title: Chief of the Dandelio Kud
states.description: Sherry is one of the two chiefs of Dandelio, a forest region inhabited by elves and kud. Though young, she is a sharp and fair leader, and also the leading expert on healing techniques and the medicinal herbs that grow in her domain.

states.title: Chief of the Dandelio Kud
states.description: The coexistence of elves and kud in Dandelio began four Pandemoniums ago, when the Dandelio kud sheltered a group of elven refugees. Now, Dandelio is a fully integrated society, and its chiefs, Sherry and Ludorié, work closely together to maintain the peace and prosperity of their homeland.

states.title: Demon Fist
states.description: Eighteen years ago, the Eternal destroyed her village. Lucija, a little girl, was the only survivor. She took two demon statues' faces, the only bit of intact art from the remains, and swore she'd get vengeance upon him.

states.title: Demon Fist
states.description: As Lucija masters the martial arts in her pursuit of vengeance against the Eternal, the demon statues speak to her and lend her power in her crusade. They've become the orphan Lucija's surrogate parents.

states.title: Bikki Sumb of Baled
states.description: Twas aeons ago, ye bikki stithered agynst devystashyn. Ye dred Jabberwock rampayged and sturmled thru ye Outbound Bayshyan. Yt whiffed thru the Memble Tree. Roro stambled be. Roro grypped his reamish blayd. Yon Jabberwock smurmured ever onwayd.

states.title: Bikki Sumb of Baled
states.description: Roro eyed ye mermy blayd but not himself's rev'rection. Verily, his ryme was in the blayd inlection. Broyed and brayed, Roro revved the blayd. He swicked ye Jabberwock's creva, and blunked yt right out the Outbound Renja. Roro became the Sumb of Baled fer evins to wumb.

states.title: Chief of the Dandelio Elves
states.description: Ludorié is one of the two chiefs of Dandelio a forest region inhabited by elves and kud. He considers his fellow chief Sherry as his dearest friend. Earnest and reliable, Ludorié feels a great deal of responsibility to his people, but is distrusting of outsiders. Those who gain his trust, however, gain a fiercely loyal ally.

states.title: Chief of the Dandelio Elves
states.description: During the previous Pandemonium, a small but influential group of humans began accusing elves of siding with demons. Many elves sought refuge in Dandelio, and Ludorié began to harbor resentment toward humans. His harsh attitude caused Dandelio to become known as unfriendly to human travelers.

states.title: The Ever-Devouring Tempest
states.description: The Kadu are a race of beings that left their homeland after devouring everything there. Ever hungry, one of their two mouths is constantly eating. This behavior often results in conflict with other species, who do not enjoy having their homes devoured.

states.title: The Ever-Devouring Tempest
states.description: An apocryphal tale say a Kadu was once confronted after eating a village. Seeing that the Kadu seemed somewhat remorseful, a clever young man proposed a deal with her—if she protected their land from invaders, she could eat the invaders, along with a certain amount of the village's food supply. This worked well for both parties, until the Kadu decided she missed the taste of village.

states.title: The Fantastic Swindler
states.description: A con artist who uses intricate illusions and sweet words to trick his foes, Ul Ganan stalks corners of the countryside where people are more susceptible to his trickery and hoodwinks them out of their valuables before disappearing into the night.

states.title: The Fantastic Swindler
states.description: Conscience is not a concept with which Ul Ganan is familiar. A successful ruse makes him feel truly alive. He developed his skills in childhood as a means of survival, but now they have become the only treasure that he truly values.

states.title: Brave Fencer
states.description: Fenn Fal was a fox who lost her mate—the love of her life, her everything. She vowed vengeance against the hunter who killed her love. However, as a fox, she couldn't face the hunter alone. So, she made a deal with a witch to merge with a dying fencer.

states.title: Brave Fencer
states.description: Ten years ago, Fenn Fal bested the hunter who killed her true love. Now, she seeks out other injustices, other tragic widows. She swears herself to their service, until such a time that vengeance has been served and she can move on to the next wronged romantic.

states.title: The Radiant Gladiator
states.description:
The youngest of a family of distinguished warriors, Vivica knew she had to do something special to get out from the shadows of her seven older brothers. When she was offered her first, child-sized sword, she wrinkled her nose and grabbed the largest greatsword she could lift.

Wielding the blade was gruelling work, but the discouragement of her family members only fuelled Vivica's fire. To their amazement, she could soon wield a blade as tall as she was as with unnatural speed.

states.title: The Radiant Gladiator
states.description:
Vivica quickly made a name for herself in local tournaments, defeating opponents twice her size.

Impressed by her power and dedication, Vivica's father offered to make her the next family head, much to her brothers' dismay. However, Vivica refused, and instead set out on a journey across Westland, determined to make her mark on the world without relying on her family name.

states.title: The Petal Paladin
states.description:
Trevyn was always a talented swordsman, a quality that made him overconfident and brash in his youth. His arrogance got him into a duel with a blind human swordmaster, but he had his world turned upside down when the elderly woman defeated him.

Believing that she had the key to something he lacked, Trevyn pleaded with her to take him on as her disciple. At first the woman would have none of it, but Trevyn's newfound humility and enthusiasm eventually won her over. From that day forth, Trevyn trained to use his sword without his eyesight.

states.title: The Petal Paladin
states.description:
Under his new master's tutelage, Trevyn quickly became adept at using his hearing to compensate for his sight. Soon he could hear a single flower petal fluttering on the wind, and then slice it cleanly into half.

But his teacher's old age soon caught up with her. On her deathbed, she confided in Trevyn that she feared her life was meaningless, as whatever she had accomplished, a swordmaster with sight could have easily done better. Trevyn assured her that her this was not the case, and swore to prove that her techniques were unrivalled. He now travels through Erden to hone his skills and fulfill his promise.

states.title: Adventure Aficionado
states.description: Solas loves training monsters, though without the influence of his fiery friend Luna he may have been content just befriending them. He enjoys his mini adventures with Luna, but he longs to go further and travel the world, certain that it must be brimming with wondrous creatures and fascinating people.

states.title: Adventure Aficionado
states.description: One evening, Luna climbed in through Solas' window and declared it was time for them to make their mark. Solas didn't much care about that part, but he wasn't one to turn down a quest, so he packed up his things, said goodbye to his mother, and set off into the night on his greatest adventure yet.

states.title: Accomplisher of Astonishing Things
states.description: Luna has always known she was destined for greatness. The moment she learned to walk she began dragging her friend Solas on grand adventures, getting the both of them into all sorts of trouble. Luna discovered she is quite skilled at catching and training monsters, and decided that must be her calling.

states.title: Accomplisher of Astonishing Things
states.description: When Luna heard that some individuals from villages in northern Rigland not far from her own home had set out on a journey to end the Pandemonium, she knew it was a sign. With Solas in tow, she set out on her own journey, ready to make the whole world remember her name.

states.title: Infernal Mercenary
states.description: The Kavaat are an ancient race often mistaken for demons. Formidable warriors that often sell their services as elite mercenaries, Kavaat are a powerful addition to any force.

states.title: Infernal Mercenary
states.description: The resemblance between sziviik and the Kavaat has led some scholars to suggest they may share a common ancestor. The fact that both groups have given the world many powerful warriors supports this theory. Unlike the sziviik, however, the Kavaat have no particular homeland, and tend to operate alone.

states.title: Bane of the Ashiran Sea
states.description: Few names inspire as much fear in sailors as "Dargarossa," the most notorious pirate to ever cruise the Ashiran Sea. With his ferocious crew in tow, Dargarossa has plundered countless ships and sunk just as many. His favorite prey are ships carrying high-class passengers, as he has an insatiable appetite for the valuable trinkets they tend to wear. As such, the nobility living along the coast have placed an exuberant reward on his squid-like head. But the local bounty hunters don't dare pursue him, as all those who have tried in the past now rest at the bottom of the sea.

states.title: Bane of the Ashiran Sea
states.description: Some say that he was formerly a normal man, cursed by the goddess of the sea for rejecting her affections. Others say he is a demon of the deep, drawn to the surface by the promise of treasure. Whatever his true nature may be, it is clear that Dargarossa has no intention of divulging it to anyone. His crew know better than to ask him about it, for those who do are sent "fishing"—they have one ankle tied by rope to the aft and are then tossed overboard, while the ship cruises at full speed.

states.title: High Angel of the Morning
states.description: Parents all across Westland tuck their children into bed with tales of guardian angels watching over their slumber. As a soldier of the Angelic Order of the Morning Light, it is Mirel's duty to protect children from ghoulish demons that prey upon their dreams. Each night, she and her comrades fight from dusk until the first rays of the morning sun.

states.title: High Angel of the Morning
states.description:
The Sky of Omens—a supernatural event of unknown origin that occurred in the skies of Erden two millennia ago. The sun vanished, and the heavens blazed in the glow of a many-colored aurora.

Without the light of morning to chase away the demons of the dusk, Mirel and her comrades found themselves unable to rest. When the soldiers around her grew hopeless, believing the dark would never end, it was Miriel who urged them on, and when the sun finally returned, she was awarded the coveted title of High Angel for her bravery.

states.title: Hero of Lu'Ganan
states.description:
Before the cycle of the Pademonium began on Erden, the ancient szi had developed an advanced civilization in the Habsu Desert.

Ja'Nafaat was the head of the temple guards of Lu'Ganan, entrusted with the protection of the faithful and their place of worship. Though he and the other guards were all well-trained, they never expected deal with anything worse than the occasional rowdy visitor.

That all changed with the onset of the first Pandemonium.

states.title: Hero of Lu'Ganan
states.description:
The invading hordes quickly devastated the szi civilization, and the survivors flocked to the temple of Lu'Ganan in hopes that their gods would provide them protection. Suddenly burdened with the task of defending these refugees from a bloodthirsty foe unlike anything they had encountered before, Ja'Nafaat felt overwhelmed.

Yet he refused to let his people and gods down. With uncharacteristic ferocity, he led the temple guards to successfully drive the enemy back after a six-day siege. The grateful refugees immortalized his name as one of the greatest warriors in szi history.

states.title: Herald of the Holy Tomorrow
states.description: Stella sees a possible future when she sleeps. During the day, she travels, teaching of this glorious time to come. She knows certain things must come to pass for it to happen, and tries to warn the world. Stella teaches of the Holy Tomorrow, and has amassed a small cult.

states.title: Herald of the Holy Tomorrow
states.description: While she cannot understand and manufacture the complex technologies of the Holy Tomorrow, she adorns herself with gadgets that mimic that tomorrowland. When she now dreams of the future, she can grab objects from the Holy Tomorrow and bring them to the present. She's made her vestment of gears and objects appropriated from her mechanical utopia.

states.title: Otherworldly War Machine
states.description:
When did the war begin? All who knew the answer have long since perished. It is a war for the sake of waging war.

In Jedariel's world, never-ending strife is the natural state of things. They are one of many soldiers, each of them perfect weapons of war. Their only goal is to take out as many enemies as possible, before their durable bodies finally fail them.

states.title: Otherworldly War Machine
states.description:
Even in a world of perfect war machines, Jedariel stood out from the rest. They could single-handedly take down dozens of other enemies, so any battle they participated in quickly became one-sided. 

It was only natural that eventually, they would draw the attention of one of the higher powers of their world. In exchange for Jedariel's utter servitude, this entity offered them even greater strength and the opportunity to use it in worlds beyond their own. Jedariel wasted no time in accepting this bargain, for there is nothing they desire more than greater power and further strife.

Now, Jedariel eagerly goes forth to defeat their master's enemies, or to die trying.

states.title: Bikki Yojimbo
states.description: An old, silent pond A frog knight draws forth his blade Nightfall in drawn blood

states.title: Bikki Yojimbo
states.description: Blade cleaned on dry grass He sheathes his only virtue A warrior's grace

states.title: Cat-Eared Mercenary
states.description:
Found abandoned as an infant near a sarru settlement, Shanna is a human child raised by sarru foster parents. The settlement treated her as one of their own, and Shanna grew up with very little interest in her human origins. She has even gone so far as to fashion a hood with cat-like ears and braid her long hair into a tail in order to make herself look more sarru. 

Obeying sarru tradition, Shanna has just begun her five-year journey to hone her combat skills as a mercenary. She hasn't had much success so far, as her shy demeanor makes her seem ill-suited for the job.

states.title: Cat-Eared Mercenary
states.description:
However, nothing could be further from the truth. Shanna has always had incredible natural talent as a fighter, able to swing around giant mauls with surprising ease despite her small stature. 

The sarru of her settlement wonder whether one or both of her birth parents might have been great warriors. Shanna may learn something about them in her travels over the next five years, but for the moment, she is perfectly content for her true parentage to remain a mystery.

states.title: Fist of the Earth
states.description: Tobias cannot remember his childhood. His earliest memory was waking one morning as a teen, and the planet telling him to fight in her defense. And so, in absence of other purpose, he's done just that. Tobias travels the world, trying to right wrongs done against the natural order.

states.title: Fist of the Earth
states.description: As humans destroy ecosystems, he returns them to the dirt as fertilizer for the next generation's wilderness. In his crusade, he's discovered that large organizations, not individuals, are the true threats to the planet. Thus, he's relearning tactics, studying how to slay bureaucracy the way he once slayed men.

states.title: Elven Bard
states.description: Velle comes from an ancient tradition of bards which teaches that stories are how cultures survive even after their people are long dead. So, she collects songs and tales from dead cities and societies. To keep stories alive, Velle travels as far as she can, and tells as many people as she can.

states.title: Elven Bard
states.description:
As Velle grows older, she wonders what will become of her own life after she passes away. Who will remember her? Who will speak of her own story?

Velle's bardic tradition forbids one from telling any tale of their own life. But nevertheless, she hopes that someday someone will craft a story about her and preserve her memory for the future, just as she is doing for tales of the past.

states.title: Principality Commander
states.description: The En are an order of Principality. Principalities watch over the peoples, protecting them from evil. They will protect that which they deem good at all costs, but they see only good and evil—no inbetween. For the people of Erden, this has made them a terrifying force, as many of their cultural icons have performed deeds that a Principality could perceive as evil.

states.title: Principality Commander
states.description: How does a society reconcile when it realizes its powerful men are not admirable? The influx of Principalities has been a mixed blessing for human cultures—though it cost them renowned leaders, it has also given them a chance to refresh their societies and reassess their values.

states.title: Szi Beast of Reckoning
states.description: According to szi mythology, the great race of mist dragons called the Zeregia herald of the end of their people. Their creation myth explains that this is why the sziviik live in the deserts—so they'll see Zeregia coming from far away.

states.title: Szi Beast of Reckoning
states.description: The sziviik developed complex aqueduct systems early in their civilization. However, they halted progress for fear that any massive body of water would invite the Zeregia, and kept irrigation and water supplies minimal. When outsiders scoff, the sziviik simply say, "We'll see who is the last standing when the Zeregia come."

states.title: Warrior of the Eagle
states.description: From a distant place, from a distant time, Zamal is a hero, a legend among his people. He fought for their freedom viciously, and welcomed glorious death with every battle. In his home, he died fighting nine gladiators. However, upon his death, he appeared elsewhere.

states.title: Warrior of the Eagle
states.description: He doesn't know why he's in Erden, except that it's tied to his skill as a warrior. Now, Zamal lives, fights, and dies time and again. Every time, he appears in another world as a liberating force, as a paragon of honorable, glorious, bloody combat.

states.title: Bikki Wanderer
states.description:
When the bikki came to Westland, they left behind much of their traditional magic in Eastland. Ambitious bikki spellcasters would attempt to make the journey back to their ancestor's homelands, in hopes of learning their forgotten secrets. The passage was long and filled with peril, and many of these pilgrims were never heard from again.

When the aspiring mage Jin decided to make the trip, his friends and family were deeply concerned. Jin's sense of direction was so terrible, it was not uncommon for him to get lost on the way to his own house. Nevertheless, Jin was too stubborn to be persuaded otherwise, and he departed for Eastland.

states.title: Bikki Wanderer
states.description:
Jin was not heard from again for over a decade, and those who knew him began to assume the worst. However, one day he suddenly returned, looking none worse for wear and adorned with fancy robes.

He had indeed reached his destination of Eastland, but not before some lengthy detours through both the northern and southern continents of Erden. As a result, he has learned far more than he would have by only visiting Eastland.

With his expanded knowledge, Jin began his own school of bikki magic, and now aspiring bikki mages seek wisdom there rather than attempt the arduous journey themselves.

states.title: Traveler of Mysterious Fortune
states.description: The graduate of a school of magic, Viola took up the mantle of her father, an archeologist who vanished under mysterious circumstances, and wanders the world researching ancient religions. Her unassuming appearance masks a proactive and eccentric mind. Though she graduated top of her class, she prefers the thrill of the unknown to badges of merit.

states.title: Traveler of Mysterious Fortune
states.description: Viola's path is ever filled with unexpected twists and turns, but somehow she emerges from any turmoil intact, and having made some kind of incredible discovery. Whether this is a special talent or just supernatural luck is unclear, but those who get mixed up in her adventures are loath to repeat the experience.

states.title: Horned God of Festivity
states.description: Jayle started as a god of festivity, living his life among the revelers and celebrants. He appeared at one party then the next, feasting on the sense of celebration and joyous abandon. He fed on the beauty of the moment, the excitement and uncertainty of superlatively salacious gatherings. And after the last Pandemonium, he fed well.

states.title: Horned God of Festivity
states.description: Surrounded by friends, Jayle eventually learned the most tragic lesson—nothing is forever. Fragile, mortal lives can be ended, and he lives on forever. When he lost one of his friends at the claws of a monster, he stopped feeding on friendship, and began feeding on the unbridled bounty of vengeance. He would punish those who destroy friendships.

states.title: Guardians of the Gate
states.description: Mezu judge the deeds of souls on the path to the underworld. This is not their will, but the will of an ancient higher power. To the Mezu, the deeds of a mortal life are inconsequential in the greater scheme of things. Their work is complemented by the Gozu, who guard the gate and enforce the decisions of the Mezu.

states.title: Guardians of the Gate
states.description: Every Mezu is charged with judging those entering the underworld. This makes for a grim outlook. So, each Mezu is sent to the mortal realm to exist for a number of years alongside the living creatures of Erden. This helps them maintain perspective on who they're judging.

states.title: Goddess of Plenty
states.description: Inanna is a goddess of abundance and renewal. However, her expression of this is unique among gods. Inanna falls out of favor with worshipers every few centuries. Yet, other religions pop up inspired by her. Inanna is a beautiful goddess, but one without pride. She lives forever in the identities of every beautiful goddess after her.

states.title: Goddess of Plenty
states.description: Every culture has a religious expression of beauty, and thus Inanna exists so long as religion exists. In the long-term, Inanna's agenda is one of dominance. She aims for ubiquity, for popularity. Even if her name is forgotten, her identity, her essence is on the tip of every culture's tongue. To her, every culture is a culture dedicated to her.

states.title: Soul of Retribution
states.description: Old elven legends speak of the Kemek, a kind of spirit that can be called to bring justice for a perceived crime. The Kemek are the driving plots of numerous stories, reassuring young elves that crimes can always be punished, but also warning of the consequences of wronging others.

states.title: Soul of Retribution
states.description: To call a Kemek, the elves say, one must draw a circle in their own blood. They sit inside the circle with a gift and state their grievance over and over until the Kemek appears. The Kemek looks over the gift, and judges its worth in respect to the justice expected of them. If they accept, they bring justice. If they refuse, they instead slay the summoner.

states.title: Jewel of the Forest
states.description:
Beryxes are elusive creatures said to live in the deepest regions of a forest. Most people know them only through a series of popular folk tales that feature Beryxes as tricksters, who fool greedy or arrogant individuals into humiliating themselves.

These tales have some basis in reality, as Beryxes are known to playfully harass travelers who become lost in the forest. In the process however, the traveler always winds up back on the beaten path again.

states.title: Jewel of the Forest
states.description:
A mature Beryx develops beautiful crystals on its body. These crystals are thought to have mystical properties. Every now and then, a foolish individual will attempt to capture a Beryx, in order to harvest these crystals for a quick profit. 

They soon discover that despite the Beryx's playful reputation, they quickly become a terrible force of nature when threatened. Beryxes will fearlessly and savagely fight anything that threatens them, even if the attacker is several times larger in size. As such, even the largest predators in the forest will step aside if they encounter one.

states.title: Elven Swordmaster Supreme
states.description: Akatsuki has trained all this life for this moment. He aims to prove himself the world's greatest swordmaster. What better chance than the Pandemonium? He's elated it happened 82 years sooner than he expected.

states.title: Elven Swordmaster Supreme
states.description: Akatsuki justifies his behavior as "good" because while he'll challenge anyone who might best him, he argues that most of those candidates are inherently evil. So, in his quest to be the best, he'll vanquish mostly evil forces.

states.title: Elven Swordmaster Supreme
states.description: Akatsuki has found a stumbling block in his quest to be the greatest swordmaster. He absolutely cannot defeat the Eternal. So, he now waits until the Crestbearer can defeat him, so he can challenge the legendary hero afterward.

states.title: The Walking Storm
states.description:
Though most people value harmony over strife, Imran was always the opposite. Even as a child, he was always caught up in some kind of fight. As soon as he was old enough to participate in wars against neighboring clans, it was clear that Imran was to become the greatest warrior of the Dust Steppes.

However, he soon tired of defeating fellow humans, so he turned to hunting down vicious monsters for a greater challenge. One day, he tracked down the whereabouts of a legendary dragon, but even he was no match against it. However, the dragon found a kindred spirit in the ever battle hungry Imran, and they agreed to have a rematch some day.

states.title: The Walking Storm
states.description:
As a sign of this promise, the dragon granted Imran small portion of its power. As a result of this boon, Imran's body now constantly crackles with electricity. Any warrior lucky enough to land a blow against him will find themselves electrocuted.

Combined with his combat prowess, Imran had now become nigh unstoppable on the battlefield. Yet he knows that he must become even stronger, in preparation for when he would meet the dragon again. For this time, the battle will undoubtedly end with one of them dying.

With a greater zeal than ever before, Imran rushes forward into conflict, striving to ready himself as much as possible before the fateful day.

states.title: The Walking Storm
states.description:
Though he would never admit it, thinking about the rematch with the dragon fills Imran with an unfamiliar emotion: fear. But it is not the fear of his own death, for he has become quite numb to that long ago. What he really fears is that he may have to kill the one being which seems to understand him.

However, to accept this would be admitting weakness, which the dragon surely would not approve of. And thus, he continues to prepare to slay the only creature that he could call a friend.

states.title: Will of the Soil
states.description: After bearing the wanton destruction wrought by the forces of the Eternal for several cycles, the soils of Erden stirred. Determined to rid themselves of the disease known as the Pandemonium, they created Vesh. Born from the earth itself, Vesh was brought forth in the height of an endless trial. It knows nothing of itself, and even the planet that bore it knows not its future.

states.title: Will of the Soil
states.description: From the moment it was created, Vesh has continued an endless quest to define itself. Through encounters both friendly and hostile it developed a personality, and arrived at a personal philosophy of sorts. It began its journey without words, but through interaction with the inhabitants of Erden, it taught itself to speak. Though it makes no effort to explain its actions, many have been saved by the swiftness of its spear.

states.title: Will of the Soil
states.description: Vesh was created to end the Pandemonium. However, it has no concept of good or evil. It merely fights because that is what it was made for, and because it believes that achieving this objective will bring it closer to the single desire it holds in its heart—to return, someday, to the beloved soil it treks across.

states.title: Scourge of the Hinterlands
states.description: Northerners warn travelers about the Grenzor, ancient beasts that must keep a fire burning deep in their hearts. They do so by devouring the warm, still beating hearts of others—the bigger the better. Though they prefer larger prey, when this becomes scarce, no living creatures, including people, are safe from them.

states.title: Scourge of the Hinterlands
states.description: Grenzor are known for grabbing travelers during snowstorms. Despite their size, they are surprisingly stealthy. As such, very little else is known about these predators, though they are commonly referenced in folklore of the north, including many stories to discourage children from misbehaving. When someone goes missing there, the locals automatically assume that they have fallen prey to a ravenous Grenzor.

states.title: Scourge of the Hinterlands
states.description: Despite the danger they pose, the Grenzor are deeply respected by inhabitants of the far north. They believe that during a Pandemonium, the Grenzor prey just as much on the Eternal's hordes as they do on innocent travellers, acting as guardians of the land against tides of demons and frenzied monsters. No one knows for sure if this really is the case, but it is true that the northeastern regions have suffered far less during prior Pandemoniums than other areas.

states.title: Bearer of the Demon Maul
states.description:
Vonn was a child of a noble elven family who was entrusted with defending their region during a prior Pandemonium.

One day, in a sealed vault deep within his father's castle, Vonn found a giant maul that had become a prison for a powerful demon lord named Drakkus. The demon promised great power to any that would wield this maul in battle, and eager to aid his father against the forces of the Pandemonium, Vonn foolishly carried the weapon out of the vault.

Just as the demon had promised, Vonn became nearly invincible in battle. Despite his father's grave concerns about the weapon, the elven forces soon came to rely on the young hero in the struggle against the Pandemonium.

states.title: Bearer of the Demon Maul
states.description:
But Vonn's heroics soon came to an end. One stormy night, the forces of the Pandemonium laid siege to Vonn's home itself. Vonn eagerly joined the fray against them with his maul, and it didn't take long for him to crush all of the attacking forces.

At that moment, the demon in the maul intentionally loosened his grip on the boy's mind, so that he could see what had really transpired. There was no siege, there were no Aeon Knights... Only his beloved family, who now lay dead and broken throughout the castle.

As the truth dawned on Vonn, his mind shattered, allowing the demon to fully claim his soul.

states.title: Bearer of the Demon Maul
states.description:
Vonn now serves Drakkus, as the demon lord seeks for a way to break his prison. Years of serving under his demonic master have corrupted Vonn beyond recognition. Gone is the naive boy who dreamed of becoming a hero like his father. Vonn is now just as cruel and heartless as the other demons under Drakkus's rule.

Yet his obedience to Drakkus is only superficial, as Vonn is still determined to avenge his family. He is merely waiting for the ideal opportunity to exact his vengeance.

states.title: Mythweaver
states.description:
The illusive Kirin are common characters in Erden mythology, but their significance greatly differs from culture to culture. The Zuo people of Eastland believe that the appearance of a Kirin is a sign that a god is near, whilst the legends of the Akashi people depict the Kirin as a symbol of great happiness.

Few have ever met a Kirin, and reports of their nature vary from gentle and majestic to divinely terrifying.

states.title: Mythweaver
states.description: An ancient folktale found among various cultural groups in Westland tells the story of a child abandoned at birth and raised by a Kirin. The child, in some tales called Enkidu and by others Eabani, grew up in the spirit world compassionate and gentle. When she turned 16, the Kirin took her back to the material realm, where she was greatly saddened by the division between its peoples. She begged her foster parent to do something, and moved by his ward's conviction, the Kirin led a procession of his breathren to thunder across the night sky.

states.title: Mythweaver
states.description:
The people of Westland looked up in wonder to see the procession of Kirin marching across the heavens. Their hooves left a trail of golden dust in their wake, which remained in the sky for seven days and seven nights after the Kirin depearted.

It is said that the scattered peoples of Ancient Westland made a ceasefire treaty effective every year for these seven days and seven nights. Many scholars equate this week of peaceful celebration with the modern day Helvati Ker festivities observed by many groups in Westland's northwest.

states.title: Riptide Guardian
states.description:
When the first Dragon of Water was slain by the Eternal, he gifted Kaidaros his power, and she used it well. It was not she who brought warfare and corruption to the sea. It was the humans, and their greed.

Their crafts started plain, barely anything more than hollow logs on the water. But as they grew ever more prideful and sailed ever further, they slew one another on her waters, hunted her ocean's bounty until entire species died. She could allow it no longer.

Her waves ripped mast from hull and dragged the villainous men to her depths. For generations, she pushed them back to their shores, until no one dared venture forth into the sea.

No one, that is, but the people who looked at her waters with awe, not avarice.

states.title: Riptide Guardian
states.description:
On Erden's widest sea, there was an island laden with wild fruit, fresh water, and good soil. Kaidaros often surfaced there when she tired of the ocean's depths, and she cared for it deeply. When she saw a ship full of humans sailing toward its shores one day, she lashed out with all her fury, desperate to keep it safe from their bloodstained hands.

But these men were not like the others. As the waves rose around them, they offered up a song of reverence to her and her domain. She had no reason to trust them. Men had begged her for their lives before...but never with such sincerity. It shook her to her core.

She could not destroy any creature that came to her in peace.

states.title: Riptide Guardian
states.description:
Through their ingenuity and patience, those first sailors would build beyond Kaidaros's island, until they built a city on the ocean itself. Kaidaros protected it as she did all her children: to the death.

She beat back many a Pandemonium from the city's walls, but even she could not best the will of a god. In the final days of an attack, she took a grave injury. At the king's behest, she retreated to the remotest reaches of the sea to rest and recover.

If she hadn't left them, perhaps they would have never fallen to an Aeon Lord's control.

But that was long, long ago, and thinking on it pains her too much.

states.title: Elmina
states.description: A mysterious elf wanted in Hymelia for theft, Elmina may seem crude and carefree, but in reality she cares deeply for others, especially children, and will put herself on the line to protect them. Her powerful magic gives her the ability to sense the presence of demons.

states.title: Theo
states.description: Mild of manner but quick of wit, Rizette's younger brother Theo is loved by all for his sweet disposition and masterful culinary skills. Though he cares deeply for his sister, secretly he longs to become more independent.

states.title: Ga'Luga
states.description:
The son of the chief of Me'Rizanté and also its strongest warrior, Ga'Luga is honorable, brave, and proud. Though he is suspicious of outsiders, if you win his trust you will struggle to find a more fiercely loyal comrade.

However, his belief that all things are solvable with brute force may land you in some sticky situations.

states.title: Mysha
states.description:
A girl from Urg Roa who lives with her grandfather, Gilgan. Bright and cheerful, and never seen without a smile. Mysha grew up admiring her grandfather and uncle, both renowned swordmasters, and idolizes Druke especially. From a young age, Mysha has trained with a sword alongside her childhood friend Ga'Luga.
Since she's not kud, Mysha grew up training alongside people much bigger and stronger than her. Still, her innate speed and agility, and her unflagging hard work, are the source of her strength.

states.title: Oz
states.description: A mysterious swordmaster who bears a striking resemblance to a certain Aeon Lord.

A guardian spirit with the power of Darkness.
Enjoys stargazing.

A guardian spirit with the power of Earth.
Enjoys tending gardens.

A guardian spirit with the power of Fire.
Enjoys hot baths.

A guardian spirit with the power of Light.
Enjoys basking in the sun.

A guardian spirit with the power of Water.
Enjoys days at the beach.

A guardian spirit with the power of the Storm.
Enjoys rainy weather.

states.title: The Cerulean Crestbearer
states.description:
"Are you sure that's not too heavy for you, Theo?"

"I'm fine, Riz. I can carry this much."

"It feels like only yesterday you were smaller than that bag."

"You're treating me like a kid again... You're not that much older than me, you know."

"That doesn't matter. You're my responsibility since Mom and Dad aren't here anymore."

"Riz... Look at the village!"

"!?"

"Those are flames!"

states.title: The Cerulean Crestbearer
states.description:
"Riz? Are you sure you're okay?"

"What do you mean?"

"With this Cerulean Crest thing... Everything happened so suddenly. How do you feel about all of it?"

"Theo... You're sweet. But I'm okay, really. I wanted the power to fight, and now I have it. And besides..."

"Besides?"

"Now Finn doesn't have to bear everything alone. He put his life on the line to help us when he didn't even know us, and he ended up with the responsibility of the Crimson Crest. If there's something I can do to lessen his burden, I'll do it."

"Riz..."

states.title: The Cerulean Crestbearer
states.description:
Dear Theo,

This is the first letter I've ever written to you, and, if you're reading it, it's also the last.

It wasn't long ago that I couldn't bear the thought of dying if it meant leaving you alone. But it's not quite like that now. I want to fight. Not just for us, but for everyone. Even if it means I don't make it to the end of the story. It's still hard to think of leaving you, but you won't be alone. I know you'll be okay. If you ever feel lost, you just need to think, \'What would Fi—

"Rizette!"

"Wah! Captain!?"

"Tell everyone to meet me outside the laboratory immediately!"

"Has there been an attack!?"

"Not yet. But there will be."

"O-Okay, I'll find Finn!"

states.title: The Crimson Crestbearer
states.description:
"Man, Finn sure is excited about this whole Lightling thing..."

"You guys're headed out again today?"

"Yep... Can't fight what's in your blood, I s'pose."

"Me missus' been saying he's looking more like his Pa every day."

"C'mon Elran, let's go!"

"Speak of the devil. I don't know how you keep up."

"I better get moving before he explodes. Keep this place safe while we're gone, boys!"

states.title: The Crimson Crestbearer
states.description:
"Hey, Callen? Just in case I don't make it through this—"

"That's not even in the realm of possibility."

"If I... If I become something that's not me anymore—"

"That's not going to happen!"

"But if it does, could you give this letter to Rizette for me?"

"I won't. I refuse to deliver another letter like that."

"Another one? Callen, were you the one who—"

"Fine, I'll keep this letter for you. But when we emerge victorious, you're going to be so embarrassed, Finn!"

"...Thank you, Callen. I'm sorry."

"Honestly! You and that idiot father of yours are two peas in a pod."

states.title: The Crimson Crestbearer
states.description:
Dear Rizette,

If you're reading this, it means our plan didn't work, and I'm not around anymore.

I still can't wrap my head around how country folk from the north like you and I got wrapped up in a wild quest with the whole of Erden on the line. If it weren't for all the people we've met, I never would have made it this far. But especially you, Rizette. Without someone else to share the responsibility of being a Crestbearer with, I don't think I could have kept going. I always find myself thinking about what you would do whenever I—

"Finn! There you are."

"R-Rizette!?"

"The Captain wants to see everyone right away. She said Callen's going to be attacked tonight!"

"What!?"

"Come on!"

"...Right behind you!"

states.title: Organica War Machine MK
states.description:
This mysterious beast resembles in form a dragon, yet it gives off no scent and hunts for no food. The weaponized attachments affixed to its body give the impression it has been artificially modified, but it bears no traces of the magical irradiation signature in other golems.

Fiercely territorial, the Orzachron utilize their mysterious and destructive weaponry to assault any who draw close.

states.title: Organica War Machine MK
states.description:
"Series IV of the OWM  represents a developmental improvement upon Series OWM III. The weakness of III, its arcane engine, has been improved with a phase space energy reactor and (unreadable). The 25mm main shaft (unreadable) a plasma striker. 
For mid-range combat, a plasma blade (unreadable) and a high vibration energy disruptor device (unreadable)." 
- Deciphered from an inscription in the remains of a Orzachron.

states.title: Organica War Machine MK
states.description:
We were born to fight. To rend skies, scorch forest, to drain the very seas and deprive our enemies of a place to call their own. That is the purpose for which we were created.
However, this world, ever-expanding before our eyes, is beautiful. What cruel irony that we should come to love it, as we can only show the depth of that love through destruction. 

Master... We were your greatest mistake.

states.title: Lady Luck's Toy
states.description:
Good Luck Baltruk.
His village razed by an iron dragon, 
the hamlet he hid in destroyed by monsters, 
the merchants who rescued him prey for tiger hornets, 
the port town where he washed ashore raided by orcs
and the village that sheltered him crushed by the Aeon Legion. 

That, is good luck Baltruk! 
This most fortunate of men entered the trials of the knighthood, 
and why did he triumph? 
Because he is good luck Baltruk, plaything of lady luck!
-From a master minstrel's song

states.title: Lady Luck's Toy
states.description:
"I thought I was just unlucky, but I was wrong. In the height of the Pandemonium, I saw countless people suffering as I once did. Feeling the same rage and sorrow I once did.

I have certainly lived through calamity, but what's key is that I lived! Now I have the chance to reach out to those in pain, to lend them what power I have and inspire them to face their misfortunes without fear!"

states.title: The Eternal Dream
states.description: Alice is a pure spirit that was born from the collective unconscious of innocent young girls. She can enter and manipulate the dreams of all living beings. Hypnos scouted her while she was playing around in people's dreams. She thinks of Hypnos as wonderland's boss, and sees it as an older friend.

states.title: The Eternal Dream
states.description: Alice spends most of her time wandering around people's dreams, and she can materialize when she's with someone who remembers their dreams. Whether in dreams or reality she is always surrounded by a forcefield called "My Little Garden", and she can make this into many forms that she can use to fight. The constantly fidgeting, sharp-tongue rabbit beside her is a product of that power.

states.title: The Eternal Dream
states.description: "Hey, you're watching me again? You know, watching someone is the same as being watched. But becoming friends? That's not so easy. That's why I want to know, and I want you to know. From when my hair was a black bob, all the way until now. All of it. Ah, but this is so frustrating. It's not like a dream at all, is it? It's like being a game where you have to follow all the rules. So what are you waiting for? Hurry and choose me to come on your next adventure!"

A genius among rabbits.
Wrote a thesis on timekeeping.

states.title: Goddess of Bounty
states.description: The Great Sage of Kisenkyo, born to a great water dragon and a human mother. Extremely skilled in onmyodo, she protects the swamplands from any who would harm its inhabitants. Her magic places intense strain on her body, and she spends most of her time resting in her pocket dimension, the Realm of Reia, or studying her magic. As a result, her social skills are somewhat atrophied.

states.title: Goddess of Bounty
states.description: Her father, a great water dragon, was once the guardian deity of Kisenkyo, but when he left, the duty fell to her. She's spent her life since then striving to protect her people, and though her devotion is respected by most, there are those who view her in a less favorable light.

states.title: Goddess of Bounty
states.description: The Great Sage, eh... aye, I've known her since I was a tadpole, and now I'm a withered old frog. She looks the same, of course... She's powerful, aye, none doubt that, and it's good to have her around...but I just can't trust her. The elders remember, and they share the tales...and now it's my turn to remember. When pressed for more details, the elder bikki simply shook his head, saying it was no matter for outsiders to hear of.

states.title: The Ladies' Knight
states.description: "Do you believe in Fate? I do. I have dreamed of this moment since I was born—but my dreams were vile slander compared to your true beauty! Oh, my Lady, in the face of your brilliance I am but a love-stricken shadow. O radiance, o poetry given form! Allow this unworthy one to compare thee to a flower..." —From \'The Love Songs of Nagi\', Vol. 3, page 12

states.title: The Ladies' Knight
states.description: "Bikki warriors are naturally blessed with martial talent! Our sensitive skin catches the slightest shift in the wind and water, while our large eyes probe even the deepest depths of darkness. My blade can sever the shadow from a sparrow on the wing! You will not escape me, blackguard!" —From \'The Heroic Tales of Nagi\', page 34

states.title: The Ladies' Knight
states.description: "Any luck on the manhunt?" "Nothing, even with the King's personal guard mobilized. What did this bikki even do?" "He single-handedly subdued that bandit group that was squatting in the Underground..." "Alone?! That man's a hero!" "...and we have 26 reports of him following women around, reading poetry at them." "...What?" —Meanwhile, outside city limits...—  "I'm afraid I have no interest in being chased around by gentlemen... but I am glad to have brought peace back to this place."

states.title: Green Dragon
states.description: Sometimes in Erden, great people reincarnate into wondrous beasts. The truly generous can sometimes resurrect as Dairyu, great green dragons. They're blessed with a unique ability to grant wishes. This makes finding them hugely desirable. However, most have expended their one wish and cannot grant another.

states.title: Green Dragon
states.description: It's said that there are magical gemstones that, when found, can restore a green dragon's wish-granting ability. The dragons vigorously deny this. Not necessarily because they don't think they exist, but because searching for a series of tiny gems all over Erden would be a fruitless exercise for even the greatest adventurers.

states.title: Lady of the White Moon
states.description:
Many old legends speak of the Lady of the White Moon, an ancient being who slumbers in a vast palace on one of Erden's moons.

It is said that if one were to visit her there, she would rise from her sleep and offer the visitor a prophecy of their future. Most of Erden's mythical heroes are said to have received advice from her, aiding them in fulfilling their grand destinies.

However, these legends offer no hints as to how to get to her palace in the first place. Various plans have been devised, such as building some kind of flying machine or creating a powerful teleportation spell, but only a handful of eccentrics have actually attempted them. Most become discouraged by recurring dreams of their plans failing, often in an extremely spectacular fashion.

states.title: Lady of the White Moon
states.description:
When Artimeia sleeps, she dreams of the future. But because the future is a tangled web of various possibilities, choices, and fates, Artimeia must sleep for ages at a time to properly understand how her actions might change outcomes. 

Thus, she tries to minimize her role as much as possible in the events that unfold in Erden, taking action only if it's absolutely necessary to ensure the world's survival. This usually amounts to only granting grains of advice to the right people at the right time. However, she understands that dire times call for more dire measures, and she will occasionally join a potential hero to help them fulfill their destiny.

states.title: Lady of the White Moon
states.description:
Artimeia does not originate from Erden—she hails from a distant world that no longer exists. Artimeia's powers of prophecy showed her the impending doom of her world, but instead of heeding her warnings, her peers exiled her as a nuisance.

Unsurprisingly, their world was destroyed shortly thereafter. Artimeia drifted through the ocean of stars in deep grief until she arrived at Erden.

In her visions, she saw that Erden's future faced many threats, and she swore to do everything in her power to safely guide this world through the dark times ahead. Learning from her prior failure, she isolated herself from the residents of Erden, hoping that subtlety will work where clear warnings had not.

states.title: Mistress of the Butterflies
states.description: Sorcerers specialize in fields. Some prefer fire. Some like healing. Some study illusions. Shirra practices insectomancy, an ancient, dead form of magic based around summoning, controlling, and speaking to insects.

states.title: Mistress of the Butterflies
states.description: In sorcery college, the other students teased her. "What use is studying bugs? You can summon termites that can eat a house in 20 years. We can summon fire that'll devour it in minutes!" Shirra would simply smile and shrug, shaking off the criticism.

states.title: Mistress of the Butterflies
states.description: Now, Shirra has become Erden's most renowned spymaster. She has a network of over 10 quintillion tiny spies, who can get into the smallest places and go unnoticed by humans, elves, and kud alike. Her services are in great demand.

states.title: Moonglow Witch
states.description: When Petra was a young girl, her mother gave her a rock she said was from the moon. Ever since, Petra's studied magic by way of moon veneration. She believes her magic will not work if she sleeps at night.

states.title: Moonglow Witch
states.description: Petra's moon magic requires sacrifice. However, this isn't sacrifice of life—it's sacrifice of sleep. She wakes others to interrupt their rest and leave them tired as part of her nightly rituals.

states.title: The Witch of Avalon
states.description: Le Fay is the leader of the nine spirits who reside in the distant isle of Avalon. She guards the Fruit of Wisdom, which bestows great knowledge to  whoever should consume it. It is said that she grants the fruit only to those who pass a series of trials. She is a cynic with a fondness for mischief who speaks in half-truths and riddles. This is part of the trials as well, as her persona helps Le Fay ascertain the true nature of those seeking the fruit she guards.

states.title: The Witch of Avalon
states.description:
Le Fay can show one who has consumed the Fruit of Wisdom what glories their future holds. However, for most this path is paved with slaughter and ends in tyranny. Once the choice is made to consume the fruit, there is no turning back.

Le Fay well knows the ultimate fate of those who choose to do so. No matter what they decide to do with their gained wisdom, they will seek to return to Avalon once more at the end of their days.

states.title: The Witch of Avalon
states.description:
Avalon is an expansive, living machine, and the nine spirits led by Le Fay are merely extensions of its will. It holds the power to produce the Fruit of Wisdom, but to do so, it demands the souls of those who die seeking its shores.

And so, all who have eaten the Fruit of Wisdom eventually find themselves returning to its source from across the seas. Those who gained the power of the fruit, and those who fell in pursuit of it. One and all, they offer themselves, body and soul, to Avalon.

states.title: Hero of the West
states.description:
"First Raid Unit. Tasks: curry favor with the Serpent Princess."
"No way they'll be assigned to the front line."
"If I could, I'd stay with this unit even when the Pandemonium is over."
"They're really just toy soldiers."
"Listen, in our case-."
"What are you doing over there
Did you not hear my summons!?"
"Apologies ma'am! We'll be there right away!"

states.title: Hero of the West
states.description:
"Your mother led battalions, Tori?"
"I heard that she fought by my father's side in the last Pandemonium."
"According to the records, they were married soon after it finished. Once you were born it seems she lead a squad against the remaining demons, but went missing. The records are short on details."
"Perhaps there was something they could not write?"
"Have you asked your father?"
"No, and I shan't. When he is ready, he will tell me."
"As you wish."

states.title: Hero of the West
states.description:
"It says here you are entering because of Commander Astrid?"
"Yes ma'am! I've been a fan since I saw her fight at Silverlodge. She's smart, graceful and super cool!"
"Hmmm."
Everyone expects great things of the commander. Luther, it would seem your daughter has exceeded even our most wild expectations. She is well on her way to becoming a legend passed down through the generations for all time.

states.title: Spirit of Spring
states.description:
I have heard many outlandish tales in my travels. In the Majas Desert, for example, the locals tell of a hidden grove where grows a tree that blooms only once every twenty years. What's more, the spirit of this tree is said to be a woman of surpassing beauty who will grant any wish made to her. A fabrication, to be sure, but a fascinating one.
(From the Chronicles of Erden, Eastland Chapter)

states.title: Spirit of Spring
states.description: I am Oumei, the spirit of the sakura. After so many years inside my tree, I've decided to finally set forth on a journey. The outside world is so vast, and every day brings new surprises, but... Oh, dear. I'm still so sleepy...

states.title: Spirit of Spring
states.description: As pleasant as my long naps are, I do grow terribly bored and lonely. And one never knows what might happen while I sleep. I hope this journey will help me find ways to protect my tree, even while I slumber. But...speaking of naps...

states.title: The Burning Hunger
states.description: The Rashanar, unlike other dragons of Erden, cannot reproduce. Rahaneer are the result of a single prehistoric event. Most fell into slumber upon this strange, magical birth, but some have awakened at separate times across the millenia. While individual Rashanar have personalities, every single one shares a hunger for fire and a want for devastation.

states.title: The Burning Hunger
