states.description: Rashanar temperament makes them incredibly dangerous, but in the hands of a master trainer, they can be harnessed as an amazing force for good. In multiple Pandemoniums throughout history, Crestbearers fought alongside trained Rashanar.

states.title: The Burning Hunger
states.description: The greatest of the Rashanar are said to be indistinguishable from gods. They're practically unstoppable, and can lay waste to massive areas on a whim. For this reason, many in Erden see untrained Rashanar as utmost threats, and devote unparalleled resources to their destruction.

states.title: Unyielding Will
states.description: The first, and only, thing to be said about Scales is how stubborn she is. She has no concept of compromise. Once she's decided on a course of action, no force on Erden can stop her, and of course she won't hear a word of advice or caution either. She didn't even consult with any of her fellow villagers before inviting herself along with me. What bad luck, to be stuck with such a hard-headed partner.

states.title: Unyielding Will
states.description: The only other thing of note about my partner is her knowledge of herbal medicine. Though limited, it did save my life once. I considered my debt repaid when I gave up my wish, but she now believes she owes me a debt instead, and has invited herself along on my journey to repay it. Perhaps we'll find a cure for stubborn foolishness along the way.

states.title: Vengeful Wanderer
states.description: The big thing about this fellow is his sharp tongue. At least he doesn't discriminate. Sziviik, kud, humans, even elves — he hates everyone equally. Every time he opens his mouth, it's insult after insult after insult. But...from what I hear, he wasn't always like this. Some things can change a man.

states.title: Vengeful Wanderer
states.description: Okay, one more thing.  His sense of direction's as twisted as his personality. He's never gotten where he's going without a guide, not once. I bet he could get lost in an empty room. He doesn't stand a chance of finding who he's looking for without my help. I sure know how to pick 'em, huh?

states.title: Knight of Vengeance
states.description:
A former knight of Hymelia, and member of the lowest rank of nobility. His father Artorias began his career as a common footsoldier, but his skills saw him promoted to General and earned him land and a noble title. Having the greatest admiration for his father, Merdain followed in his footsteps, and he quickly became a very promising knight, despite the derision he endured for his low rank and common origins.
However, the nobles of the Senate conspired against Artorias, and he fell on the battlefield, leaving Merdain torn between his loyalty and vengeance.

states.title: Knight of Vengeance
states.description:
On Avalon, Merdain learned that his father took advantage of an unnatural power, the Fruit of Wisdom. The realization that even his father, with all his resourcefulness, loyalty, and hard work, had to resort to supernatural power to be acknowledged by the nobility of Hymelia sank Merdain into a deep despair.
What good is loyalty to a country that despises you?

states.title: Knight of Vengeance
states.description:
Merdain's rage is directed not only at the nobles who assassinated his father, but the whole of the Kingdom of Hymelia and their system of blood lineage, nobility, and titles. With the power of the Fruit of Wisdom, he should be able to change the kingdom... by burning it down and building something new atop the ashes.
He knows that only tragedy lies down this path, but he's prepared to sacrifice everything, even his own life, for the sake of his revenge. Everything, except perhaps for one...

states.title: Fighter Against Fate
states.description:
A knight of Hymelia. Unlike his childhood friend Merdain, he comes from a high-ranking, long-standing noble house. He was sickly and weak as a child, but he forced himself to keep up with his friends Merdain and Maxima, overcame his weakness, and is now an outstanding knight.
Lucius strongly respected Merdain's father Artorias, who rose from the rank of footsoldier to the rank of General, and then to nobility. He sees Artorias as proof that anything is possible if you work hard, and believes he can overcome his illness too if he only works hard enough.

states.title: Fighter Against Fate
states.description:
Maxima: "First one to the big oak is the winner, okay? Ready, set, go! ...Haha, I win again! You're so slow, Lucius!"

Merdain: "Lay off, Maxima. Last month he couldn't even keep up with us, remember?"

Maxima: "Hmph. You always take Lucius's side."

Lucius: "Sorry, I just... need to catch my breath. Can we rest for a minute?"

"Merdain: Knights don't get to rest on the battlefield! Come on, get up! We're doing best of three!"

states.title: Fighter Against Fate
states.description:
Lucius arrives on Avalon with Merdain, and they learn the truth about Merdain's father. However, it doesn't change Lucius's respect for Artorias. He believes that Artorias would have distinguished himself even without the Fruit of Wisdom.

Artorias was kind and supported Lucius when he was starting out as a knight, and he served as Lucius's inspiration to better himself. Partially in order to repay that debt, Lucius wants to protect Merdain from his own self-destruction and rescue him from ending up as food for Avalon. He'll face any foe, even the gods themselves, to keep that from happening.

With a strong enough will, even fate can be changed, or so he believes.

states.title: Bearer of Corpses
states.description:
One of the Oracles, the nine spirits who protect and oversee Avalon. Her main job is to retrieve the corpses of those who return to Avalon, and unlike her elder sister Le Fay, she doesn't often interact with living humans.
She cares the most about keeping the peace on the island, and if outside invaders ever arrive on Avalon, she's the first one to engage them in battle. Despite her slender build, she is monstrously strong. Generally speaking, the other eight Oracles never even have a chance to fight before the battle is over.

states.title: Bearer of Corpses
states.description:
"What, pray tell, is he doing?"
"He says he is building a raft. It is amusing, watching him struggle with the trees and vines."
"Amusing enough to watch for a whole afternoon? He rejected the Fruit. He's unworthy, and you're wasting your time."
"Really? Perhaps we are mistaken about that. Perhaps I should observe him further to be sure, even across the ocean..."
"...Le Fay, you..."
"Be at ease, dear sister. It was only a joke."
"...If you're sure."

states.title: Bride of the Sea
states.description: The young Queen of the island nation of Laomis, and head priestess of Nautica, the Primordial of Water. The people of Laomis depend entirely on the sea for their lives, so they view Esma, whose job it is to keep the favor of the tempestuous Primordial, with the utmost respect. Though she's a fine ruler and well-liked by her people, she's still very young, and sometimes feels like she'll buckle under the weight of her responsibilities.

states.title: Bride of the Sea
states.description:
Though a demure and gentle ruler now, Esma used to be quite a tomboy, sneaking out of the palace to go down to the docks, listen to the pirates' tales of their exploits, and dream of the world on the other side of the ocean.
\Over the course of her visits, she fell in love with a dashing young pirate; but he refused her feelings. Heartbroken, in a moment of weakness, she prayed to Nautica for revenge... and upon hearing that his ship had been lost to the sea, she realized that she had done something unforgivable. The regret still torments her, but the harsh lesson helped shape her into the responsible monarch she is today.

states.title: Hero of Rigland (On Break)
states.description: "Ludmilla was very insistent I come here, but what exactly does one do on "vacation"? There's nothing here but sand and seawater. The sun is too bright, the sand is too hot, and... I can't believe I'm dressed like this outside my bedchamber. And yet... somehow, I feel..."

states.title: Hero of Rigland (On Break)
states.description:
Mysha: I wonder how Astrid's doing.
Theo: She doesn't seem like the vacation type, does she?
Rizette: Don't worry, I'm sure she's having a great time! I wish I could have gone, too...
Mysha: Once things settle down, why don't we all go to the beach together?
Nagi: Yes! A thousand times, yes! By all means, let us all go together!
Rizette: ...I'll go if Nagi stays here.
Nagi: Wha... h-have a heaaart! Waaaah!
Theo: He's bawling?!

states.title: Hero of Rigland (On Break)
states.description:
"I've spent my whole life following in my father's footsteps. I learned of duty and responsibilty before I could walk. The mantle of the commander of the Red Dragon Knights, the title of "Hero of Rigland"... I never realized how heavy they were before. If I'd kept shouldering them, sooner or later I would be crushed under the weight. 
Thank you for giving me this respite, my friends. I'll have to pick up that weight again soon... but for right now, I'll let the waves carry me."

states.title: Summertime Valkyrie
states.description:
"...Marshal? What are you doing now?"
"Ah, Lady Astrid! This is a sport known as 'surfing'. You stand on this board-like thing, and use it to ride the waves!"
"You certainly seem to be enjoying yourself."
"Yes, of course! Would you like to join me? It makes for a fine way to train your balance!"
"I-I'll pass, thank you."
(The Marshal doesn't seem to draw a line between training and recreation...)

states.title: Summertime Valkyrie
states.description:
Laomis: the glimmering pearl of the South Seas. Every Riglander dreams of visiting this tropical paradise at least once, and the Red Dragon Knights' hard-headed Marshal is no exception. However, with all of her responsibilities, it seemed like a faraway dream within a dream. She had halfway given up when Queen Esma's invitation descended like a gift from on high.
"I cannot afford to fail on this mission. I must enjoy myself to the fullest...!"
Vacation is no excuse to slack off, after all.

states.title: Summertime Valkyrie
states.description: Even with the Pandemonium ended forever, Ludmilla has no time to rest. Supporting her young Commander, punishing bandits and looters, destroying any remaining monsters... Her days are packed to the brim, and she wouldn't have it any other way. Still, taking rest when you can is a basic rule of battle, and Ludmilla knows that very well. Improving oneself has no point if you run yourself into the ground to do it. So, she pushes herself to her limits, rests, and then pushes herself to her new limits once again. Tomorrow she takes up her mantle and sword once more; tomorrow she must push herself once again, so today, she rests with all her might.

states.title: Blue Hurricane
states.description:
Despite her young age, Adena is the first mate and right hand of Dargarossa, the fearsome Bane of the Ashiran Sea. While on her way to be sold as a slave in a far-off land, her ship was raided by Dargarossa and she was freed; with nowhere else to go, she decided to stick around, and became a pirate. Though free-spirited, she respects Dargarossa deeply.
When the call to board is sounded and the battle is joined, she's the first one across the gap, leaping to the deck of the other ship and laying waste around her with her spear. Despite her young age, the crew respects her second only to the Captain himself, and they call her "Sis".

states.title: Blue Hurricane
states.description:
Cap'n, it weren't me what hit you with the ball! It was Sis! C'mon, Sis, back me— huh?! Where'd she go?!
"......"
"C'mon, Cap'n, calm down! Huh? Yer gettin' a bump on yer head? Nah, that's just what yer head always looks—Aaaaugh!"
Splash!
"Whew, he went far! Might be a new record. Ah, hey, land ho! Someone haul that sorry carcass outta the drink and make ready for landfall!"

states.title: Fiery Princess
states.description:
The eldest daughter of the Hymelian royal family. Though a princess, she is strong-willed and a highly skilled warrior, so she often fights on the front lines. The pride of the Hymelian Knights, Merdain, can just about hold his own with her. Their other childhood friend, Lucius, has practically never beaten her in any contest.
The nobility and conservative voices in government disapprove of her un-princesslike behavior, at least behind closed doors, but soldiers and the common people adore her.
In every generation, at least one member of the Hymelian royal family has a contract with the primeval fire spirit Volcana. Currently, that responsibility falls to Maxima. Volcana has a particular soft spot for the gallant and brave Maxima, and Maxima views the flame spirit less as an object of worship, and more as a sometimes harsh, but loving mother.

states.title: Fiery Princess
states.description:
Maxima was quite a tomboy in her youth, and she would sneak out of the palace to play with her friends Merdain and Lucius. She was repeatedly told not to associate with Merdain because of his low standing, but rather than obey, she wondered why people had to be so cruel to one of her best friends.
Spending time outside the palace, Maxima had many chances to interact with common-born soldiers and smallfolk, and she began to question the nobility's focus on bloodline. Why should the house someone is born into dictate their worth in life? Eventually, she realized that she wanted to change her country.

states.title: Fiery Princess
states.description:
Her father the King is a good man, but weak-willed, serving as the puppet of the nobles of the Senate. Maxima is third in line for succession to the throne. If she can convince her two older brothers to see her way, then all is well, but if not, she is prepared to fight. To that end, she is solidifying her connections within the army. If she has to, she'll even bring Merdain and Lucius and take the throne by force.
While she was planning, however, a tragedy occurred. The Senate successfully arranged Merdain's father Artorias's death in a futile war, and Merdain and Lucius went missing as well. If only she had acted more quickly...but she knows that if she had enacted her plans prematurely, the kingdom could have very well shattered under the strain.

states.title: Void Renegade
states.description:
Every thief comes with her own sad story, but Elmina's is especially heinous. Living as a street urchin in Hymelia, the sweet words of a Gru Grimm preacher drew her in. He promised he could numb her pain, erase her history, and give her the power to do as she pleased, as long as she would swear her life to his mysterious god. She did -- and soon came to find that in doing so, she swore away her very free will.

Nowadays, she'd sooner swallow coals than follow orders. Sure, not everyone in authority is as evil as the cult that stole her youth, but she isn't taking any more chances. She is more than happy to help her friends -- but she will never spill blood again for what she doesn't believe is right.

states.title: Void Renegade
states.description:
"Elmina, Elmina! I patched the hole in your shirt for you!"

"Aww, kiddo. You didn't have to, I was going to take care of that tonight myself."

"I know. But you've been so busy with your new friends. I thought you'd want to spend some more time with them instead of doing chores at home."

"Honestly, doing chores at home sounds like a dream right now. How do you think my shirt got a hole in the first place?"

"Were you playing too hard with your friends?"

"Sure. We were playing. With pointy sticks and monsters. Speaking of which, never go out at night without me, okay?"

states.title: Void Renegade
states.description:
She shouldn't hold herself responsible for what she did under the Gru Grimm's control. She knows this. The Red Knights seem to know it too, since they haven't seized her and thrown her in prison with the other leaders of the Gru Grim.

But still, late at night when sleep eludes her, Elmina's mind wanders back to the faces of those she's killed. The fear in their eyes. The blood on her blade. Each life taken is a grievous wrong that can never be undone. Whether her hands were free or pulled on puppet strings makes no difference.

Aiding children and doing heroics with her friends might not be enough to completely fix the world she once helped to break. But it flies in the face of the Gru Grimm's old commands, and for her, that is enough.
A mirage created by Elmina to distract her enemies.

states.title: Born of Winter
states.description:
Born at the height of a historic snow storm, Yuki has always had coldness flowing through her veins. As a baby, she would summon snow flurries to rock her own cradle. As a child, she would freeze a nearby lake to go ice skating at the height of summer. Her friends and family marveled at her powers...for a time. But the human heart is a fearful and jealous one, unable to abide what it can't understand. On the eve of her eighteenth birthday, the village elders exiled her, swearing to her a swift sword to the neck should she return.

Their mistake was in thinking that she'd ever want to.

states.title: Born of Winter
states.description:
Yuki made work for herself as a wandering mage for several years. She flitted from noble to noble who wanted the services of the famed Winter Witch, either for entertainment or for war. The spectacle of animating ice sculptures and burying opposing armies under snow was amusing, but without fail, she would grow bored with her master's mundane tasks and set off to find another. Erden, it seemed, held little of interest to a living force of nature. That is, until the Pandemonium began.

Rody and his friends approached her with neither fear nor gold, only a request that she use her immense power to help slay the Eternal. The prospect intrigued Yuki enough for her to comply.

If she'd known how their quest would end, she'd never have dared.

states.title: Born of Winter
states.description:
Eighteen years later, the Winter Witch is a legend whispered in the dark, never too loudly, lest they attract her attention and potentially, her ire.

They say she lives in a mountain of ice, freezing the blood of all who seek her out.

They say her storms shatter castles like glass.

They say that she is winter's mad queen, another curse upon the world, filling the void left by the end of the Pandemonium.

They do not know that she is merely Yuki, heartbroken by the fate of her only friends, lashing out at a world she has never understood.

states.title: Flame Incarnate
states.description:
Long ago, in the time of the first Pandemonium, Fornaxos inherited the power of flame and became one of the six Legendary Dragons that embody the elements of Erden. Tasked with defeating the Eternal, he knew that untempered fire would destroy more than it would save. In his humility, he retreated to a lone mountain to hone his power and train for the battle to come.

Settlers who ventured too far up that mountain would return bearing tales of a shining creature who split the night sky with pillars of flame. Thus, the villagers came to call their home Mount Fornax, in honor of the dragon who shared it with them. Most have now forgotten why the rocky peak bears that name...but just enough of the story survives to inspire travelers to make the climb, in the hope that they'll see a legend in the flesh.

states.title: Flame Incarnate
states.description:
Fornaxos spent centuries alone, preparing himself to one day face the godlike Eternal. Now that the threat is vanquished, he finds himself a bit perplexed at his existence. If his purpose was to end the Pandemonium, and a group of mortals completed that purpose for him, what right does he have to call himself a legendary guardian of Erden?

After some thought, he has decided to leave the lonely summit of Mount Fornax to explore the world beyond. His brethren, the five other Legendary Dragons, must be facing the same conundrum that he is, now that the world is free. Perhaps with them by his side, he can forge a new legend to fulfill.

states.title: Flame Incarnate
states.description:
Though he is a being of nearly unrivaled power, Fornaxos deeply admires the mortal people of Erden. Their fortitude in the face of the world's disorder astounds him. Death could come for any of them at any time, and their lives are too short to see their present toil bear fruit in the future. Even still, they create. They love. They live, knowing full well that one day they will be no more. To him, that is strength that a god could never hope to possess.

Many of Erden's civilizations have disappeared in his lifetime, consumed by their own mortality. Sometimes, when he's feeling nostalgic, he will leave an offering in their ruins to prove to their wandering spirits that their short, brilliant lives are still remembered, if only by a single soul.

states.title: Flametongue Maid
states.description:
During the third Pandemonium, the legendary Fire Dragon Fornaxos saved a human man from certain death. To show his eternal gratitude, the man swore that his bloodline would stand guard over and sanctify Fornaxos's mountain until the end of days. Thus, Antares' ancestor decided her fate centuries before she was born.

She doesn't begrudge him this, of course. Antares has always been happy to have a purpose, even if it was one she never chose herself. Still, some days she finds her eyes wandering to the village at the foot of the mountain, far from the sacred ground she cares for all alone.

Just who are these people that Fornaxos works so hard to protect?

states.title: Flametongue Maid
states.description:
Now that Fornaxos has freed her of her ancestor's vow, Antares finds herself fascinated by the many peoples and cultures of Erden. Like a firefly on the breeze, she flits from place to place, learning all she can.

As a former recluse, however, she never had to learn to tame the considerable fire magic within her. This has caused its fair share of difficulties in her travels. For instance, one bikki village forbade her to return after she dried up all its mud baths in a single day.

If you know any tutors of fire magic that don't mind getting a few burns, please let her know.

states.title: Fledgling Shadow
states.description:
"Bad boy, Crescent! Bad! Bad!"

"Kyuu..."

"What'd the dragon do this time?"

"I hope you didn't want dinner tonight, Solas, because our eggs have vanished."

"What do you mean? Did they roll out of your basket again?"

"Of course not! I was just putting the pepper on my omelette when Crescent sneezed on it, or something. This black stuff came out of his mouth instead of fire --"

"Black stuff? Like tar?"

"No, like a shadow. Or a bit of night. I don't know. Anyway, it hit the eggs and made them disappear, so Crescent's not getting any more grapes for a week. Hmph."

"Kyuu..."

states.title: Fledgling Shadow
states.description:
"Hey, Luna. Can I talk to you about Crescent?"

"Okay...but why? I thought I finally got him housebroken."

"You really haven't, but that's not why I'm worried. You know that shadow stuff he can breathe when he's surprised or angry?"

"Yeah?"

"I just found a book about dragons at the library. I tried to look up Crescent's species, but there wasn't a single mention of dragons who could breathe shadow. Not anywhere."

"Really? That's unbelievable..."

"I know. Given where we found him, we have to consider that Crescent might not be a normal --"

"Why would a library keep such an incomplete tome of monsters on its shelves?! Such unprofessionalism! I'm going to lodge a complaint."

"Luna..."

states.title: Crimson Valkyrie
states.description:
Among the noble houses of Rigland, House Aldyne boasts one of the longest and proudest pedigrees, and some of the fiercest warriors ever to face the Pandemonium.
Ludmilla, the family's only daughter, was raised to be demure, retiring, and modest; but her family's blood burned strong in her.
A noble soul from birth, she stood up against injustice, even to adults, and protected the weak when they could not protect themselves, despite her parents' protests.
Somehow, it still came as a surprise to her family when she ran away from her sheltered life, joined the Red Dragon Knights, and stepped into the history books as a champion.

states.title: Crimson Valkyrie
states.description:
"Hey, Boss! We got trouble! The Red Dragon Knights were spotted in a village nearby!"
"Calm down. We just need to make ourselves scarce before—"
"Too late! Throw down your weapons, bandit scum!"
"What?! The Crimson Valkyrie?!"
"But how?! That village is hours from here!"
"Unfortunately for you, we run this far before breakfast every morning. In armor. Isn't that right?"
"R-right, Captain..."
"Damn...we'll have to fight! They'll be tired from the march! Kill 'em!"
"How considerate! We won't have to chase you down. Red Dragon Knights, draw arms!"

states.title: Crimson Valkyrie
states.description:
"The Pandemonium is over. Surely you've done enough? Why not retire?"
"It's about time you found a husband, dear. We've received so many excellent proposals for you!"
"Father. Mother. There are more dangers to Erden than the Pandemonium alone. There is still much to be done on the path to peace."
"Don't be foolish. People cause conflict; that's the way of the world. There will never be peace."
"If Justice is served with honor and humility, someday evil will be eradicated from this world. My comrades all believe that."
"Do you mean to see our bloodline perish on the end of some brigand's dagger?!"
"My blood, our blood, cries out within me, Father. 'Protect the weak. Slay that which is evil and injust.' If I turn a deaf ear to that voice, the Aldyne line dies then and there. I may live, but my spirit will be dead. Is the continuance of our name worth the forfeiture of our heart and soul?"
"Ludmilla..."

states.title: Blade of the Aeons
states.description:
"So! You're that "demon" Norza that's been causing trouble around here!"
"...And you are?"
"They call me Matias... Matias the Flamebearer! I hear you've been seeking out skilled warriors, boy. Well, your predations end here and now! Draw your sword. I challenge you to—gaaaah!"
"Spare me the speech. If you mean to kill, do it quickly and quietly...mm. I guess you can't hear me anymore."

states.title: Blade of the Aeons
states.description:
The young man traveled the land, seeking the strongest warriors he could find.
As he was already a peerless swordsman, most duels ended quickly. On the rare occasions he was at a disadvantage, he would retreat and return again the next day, until he won.

Those he defeated, he slew without mercy. True power can only be found beyond the boundary of life and death.
Standing over the bodies of his opponents filled him with a rush of power.

Still not enough. I can get stronger still. I must get stronger still. I must defeat stronger foes.
The avarice in his heart drew him ever onward, fleeing from the powerlessness he felt that day.

states.title: Blade of the Aeons
states.description:
Finally, there were none left in Westland to challenge Norza.
His final target was the Eternal: the ultimate foe that not even legendary heroes had vanquished.
He sought the dormant Eternal, and whether by Fate or by the abomination's will...he found it.
Even against the dormant Eternal, Norza stood no chance.
As he fell into death's embrace, the Eternal offered him a choice:
Die as a human, and rot into forgotten nothingness;
or swear fealty, and be reborn as an Aeon Lord.
He recalled Argento's face. The sweet temptations of that demon blade.
He didn't hesitate for even a second.

Reborn as the Pristine Corpseblade, he wielded immense power...and the blessing of Eternity.
Never again would he have to fear death, to die ingloriously like "she" did...

...And before long, he had forgotten who "she" even was.

states.title: Apex Blademaster
states.description:
Druke's father was a skilled blacksmith, his name known across South Rigland.
Warriors would come from all over, crossing the brutal Habsu Desert just to buy his wares.
However, his son possessed no skill for the forge at all.
Though he worried for his son, the blacksmith took a local boy, Gilgan, as his apprentice and successor, and Druke lived his teenage years shiftless and ill-content.
However...one night, a bandit snuck into the forge while the boys' parents were out.
Gilgan advised caution, but Druke seized one of his father's blades from the wall and struck the thief down.
It was as if Druke had cut the haze from his thoughts with the same blow.
Though his parents scolded him, Druke felt certain. Forging weapons was not the only way to live by the sword.

states.title: Apex Blademaster
states.description:
Rody: "We'll need to do something about that dragon if we want to get through here. What's the plan?"
Callen: "I have an idea. I can concoct some sleeping medicine. If we put it in some meat and leave it out as bait..."
Druke: "That'll take way too long. I say we run in there and kill the stupid thing."
Callen: "Don't be ridiculous! That thing will tear you to—"
Rody: "No, he's got a point... we are in a hurry, after all. Let's not overthink this."
Callen: "Not you too!"
Rody: "Look, it's not even a dragon god, it's just a regular dragon. If we can't kill it, how can we hope to fight the Eternal?"
Callen: "...Well...I mean... Yuki, say something to these idiots!"
Yuki: "Just a flying lizard. It'll be no match for my ice."
Callen: "You... you meatheads!"

states.title: Apex Blademaster
states.description:
The Eternal was defeated, and peace returned to Erden. Amidst the celebration, Druke fell into black despair.
The vow he made to Rody that day in Urg Roa...

"I swore to protect you, no matter what. But I failed...and you're gone.
All our friends have gone their separate ways, to their separate futures. But what is there for me?"

"This peace is only temporary. When the horror of the Pandemonium fades, people will turn on each other again.
What a senseless world...but it's the world my friend gave his life to save.
So I'll protect it instead. This time, I'll fulfill my vow. As my atonement."

"I need power. Absolute power, to crush any foe, and keep my people safe.
Some nights I hear that beast, the one the Aeon Lord showed me. I feel its roar deep inside.
But it's of no matter.
I have work to do."

states.title: Storm-Inspired Inventor
states.description:
Greta is going to change the world someday. Everyone in her village told her so when she was a child, so it must be true. She has everything a visionary needs: a whip-smart mind, a tireless work ethic, and perhaps most importantly, a total lack of common sense. She dreams of one day capturing lightning in a bottle, so that the people of Erden can have as much light as they want, whenever they want.

A traveling Winvy merchant -- who has gone by many names, but who she knows as Mr. Beauregard Chancy -- saw the potential of her idea when she chased him down at the market fair one day. He agreed to put her through sorcery school in exchange for the exclusive right to sell her invention once she has a working model.

It's been three years. It must be coming any day now.

states.title: Storm-Inspired Inventor
states.description:
There's nothing Greta loves more than a good thunderstorm. She goes out in them to take notes whenever she can; she'll drag herself out in her pajamas, if she has to. You must first understand something to tame it, and after three years, Greta understands lightning as much as anyone can. Still, the pouring rain and rolling thunder relax her, so she continues studying anyway.

Storms have always been her friend. Once, when she was very young, she fell asleep reading by candlelight. The wax dripped onto her straw bed, and in minutes, her home went up in flames. Only a passing thundercloud saved her family's lives. She'll never forget how bright the lightning was that split the sky that night.

She's chased it ever since.

states.title: Champion of the Black Flames
states.description:
The servants of Orcanix know no death. Should they be reduced to dust, their master will summon them to her Pyre and create their bodies anew. Even so, nothing can be destroyed again and again without consequence.

The Paladins have no flesh, no blood. Some have lost even their bones, existing more as ghostly wraiths than moving corpses. They would wear their twisted husks with pride, if they could feel such things. But rebirth demands a price far beyond the physical, and they have paid in full.

Nothing remains inside them but bloodlust and blind obedience.

states.title: Champion of the Black Flames
states.description:
The armies of Orcanix revere their Paladins almost as much as they revere the goddess herself. Some have existed for centuries, before the war with the mortal kingdoms. Some have shed the blood of kings, some have slaughtered dragons. All leave legends in their wake.

No coward can become a Paladin. They are tempered by death as steel is tempered by flame. To be a Paladin is to embrace suffering, to welcome and inflict it in equal measure. The goddess lets her monsters lead Erden's conquest, and they have never left her wanting. But she keeps her most elite for a secret purpose, far beyond what her underlings can fathom.

states.title: Soul of the Storm
states.description:
When the Primordial Dragon of Storm fell in the first battle with the Eternal, he died too quickly to choose his successor. Desperately, he let a portion of his power fly into the world, to choose and grow within whatever it might find. It was reckless. It could spell disaster if the power of the heavens landed in the wrong hands. But he had no other choice.

As it happened, a hatchling crawled out of his cave to hunt for scraps. He had been cast away from his brethren, too small and skittish to call himself a dragon. Had he hid a moment longer, he might have died a runt, forgotten and inconsequential. But that was not to be his fate.

Altaireon stepped beneath the open sky, and the power of the storm struck him like a bolt of lightning.

states.title: Soul of the Storm
states.description:
For many years, he resented and feared his new powers. He could barely control the thunder in his veins, and worse, he had done nothing to earn it. But after a century of fleeing the storms that dogged his steps, Altaireon decided he could run no more. Erden cried out for a savior to break the brutal cycle of the Pandemonium. He was not worthy to be that savior -- not yet. But he could become worthy. He owed it to the heavens that had gifted him their fury.

The young Legendary Dragon sharpened his claws and got to work.

Success did not come quickly. A storm resists taming by its very nature; if he isn't worthy, even a dragon will find himself at the mercy of the winds. After months of fruitless effort, Altaireon found a group of mortal monks living secluded in the mountains. Though they had no power of their own, and no assurance of immortality, they sought whatever perfection they could achieve in their lives. Some wrote poetry, others performed martial arts. Some only meditated from dusk until dawn, pondering the reason for existence. These were not the cowardly mortals Altaireon had seen rise since the fall of the dragons. These were a cut above the rest. In some ways, they were even more than him.

He humbly submitted himself to their teaching, and though he lived to see the youngest of them wither and die, he could not have mastered the tumult of the storm without them.

states.title: Soul of the Storm
states.description:
In the centuries to follow, Altaireon would push himself beyond what he ever thought possible. Armed with lightning and armored with wind, no enemy could stand against him. Battle became so trivial that he gave up walking the earth entirely, preferring to soar alone and undisturbed. He would only land for those he thought might pose a challenge. They very rarely did.

He does remember one, however. A mortal hunter, wild-eyed and thirsty for glory, who shot him down as he was circling a mountaintop. The two fought ferociously, and though Altaireon emerged the victor, the hunter dodged a bolt of lightning to deliver one last blow with his dagger. So impressed was the dragon that he gifted the hunter a small fraction of the storm, as he had once been gifted himself. He then bid the mortal to train himself and return only when he was certain he could take a Dragon Lord's life.

Altaireon doesn't know if the hunter will ever succeed in his task. It has been many years now, and he has heard nothing of him. But he suspects it won't be long.

The mortal heart doesn't easily give in.

states.title: Angel of Ruin
states.description:
She doesn't know how many times she has died. They all blur together, a haze of pain, darkness, and brutal rebirth. There's no point in remembering them all, not when she'll live until the end of time.

But there was one death that mattered. Her first death. The one that should have been her last. She would give anything to forget, but it burns like a torch in her mind, as eternal as her master's black fire.

If Finn shares her tormented memory, he has never shown it. Beneath his twisted shell, he might have no memory at all.

states.title: Angel of Ruin
states.description:
None of them knew it would go so wrong.

The odds were always stacked against them. But Rei had gifted them her magic. Callen had forged their hero a holy sword. The people of Erden cheered for their victory, sending them off to the Endless Pyre with celebrations and songs.

For a time, they thought they had a chance.

They were fools to ever think so.

Gods are gods and men are men. Even with a legendary blade, Finn was doomed to fall. Looking back, Rizette wonders if she should have stood her ground. She wonders what might have happened if she hadn't given her life for his.

Would he still have betrayed them all?

states.title: Angel of Ruin
states.description:
The pain of the goddess's talon in her heart remains. The warm taste of blood remains. Finn's anguished screams remain. But the memory of death -- true, mortal death -- fades more and more each day.

She can call it back a little, when she's alone on quiet nights. A thousand voices assured her that her sacrifice was not in vain. A warm light shone around her, gentle as a mother's embrace.

The only light she knows now is the light of the Endless Pyre.

states.title: Yuletide Rogue
states.description:
"Hey! Watch where you're going, you nearly made me drop my stuff!"

"What are you rushing around for, anyway? All the good stuff in this market's already been snatched up. Don't tell me you're one of those people who put their shopping off to the last minute."

"...sigh. Of course you are. I should have guessed."

"Well, don't just stand there. Follow me. I know a couple places that can help you out, but you better not do this again, you hear? The people in your life must really care about you. What's the point of Christmas if you don't give them something special?"

states.title: Yuletide Rogue
states.description:
"Merry Christ -- what? You again? How'd you find out where I live?! If you followed me home, I swear..."

"One of the kids let you know, huh? I keep telling them not to bother with strangers, but they never seem to listen."

"Hey, you weren't a stranger, you were -- a charity case! You were wandering around so lost, it would have been bad karma to leave you on your own! Anyway, why did you even come here?"

"...to give me a thank you present. Yeah, right. There isn't a thing in the city left to buy."

"Huh? You made it yourself?"

"..."

"Guess it'd be even more bad karma to turn you away now. Fine, fine, come in. But watch where you're going. I didn't buy all these gifts just to have 'em stepped on."

states.title: Yuletide Rogue
states.description:
"Hey. Sorry to bother you so late at night."

"How did I find your place? Easy. I followed you. You're not hard to miss."

"...I know. I'm a horrible hypocrite, aren't I? But I didn't want the night to pass without paying you back for earlier."

"I don't like leaving people hanging. And I thought you might be going home lonely. Holidays can get pretty quiet once the parties are over."

"N-not that I'm inviting myself in. I just brought you one last Christmas present. That's all."

"...I can stay as long as I want?"

"..."

"Thanks. I think I'd like that.""Merry Christmas. Now shut the door before you let in the cold."

states.title: Timeless Beauty
states.description:
As the avatar of Chronos, Rolotia dutifully watches over the year as it passes away. She feels the weariness of the land, strewn with bare forests and blanketed under snow. She sleeps by the side of hibernating animals, holed up in their dens. She knows, as the world does, that the end will soon arrive.

Quiet as a whisper, the world will begin anew. She will see this year from its winter birth to the heights of its summer, and though it too will die someday, she greets its first day with a smile.

states.title: Timeless Beauty
states.description:
Many people start the new year with self-reflection. With resolute hearts, they set out to be new people, free of the vices that have held them back. Rolotia observes these resolutions with glee, even knowing most of them will be broken. The first days of a year have a purity to them, a power that makes any dream appear within reach. If a single person sees their promises through, none of their failed resolutions were wasted.

She's no stranger to wanting a second chance.

states.title: Timeless Beauty
states.description:
"Sorry to keep you waiting! I made us some snacks. If it's not too cold, why don't we sit outside for a while?"

"I got everyone's new year's cards this morning. I must admit, I was a little surprised. I've welcomed in so many new years all alone. To think so many people are keeping me in their hearts today..."

"Oh, but I'm being sentimental. Here. I sent out all the others, but I wanted to give this one to you myself."

"May the year be kind to you, dear friend."

states.title: Snowkissed Maid
states.description:
When she set out on her mercenary journey, Shanna thought she would only learn the ways of combat. As it happens, she's discovered Christmas too.

The human holiday mystifies her a bit. The saru are a generous people, but they are also practical. They exchange gifts all year long with little fanfare. So why do humans tie them up with bows and leave them under a tree? Why do they build cookie houses without eating them? Why do people gather to sing songs in the snow? Aren't they cold?

There's much she still needs to learn, but she thinks she likes this strange celebration of theirs.

states.title: Snowkissed Maid
states.description:
"Oh, you made it! Welcome!"

"Do you want some eggnog? A candy cane? How about a gingerbread man?"

"The market didn't have any more turkies, so I caught one myself! Did I make it right? It's over there, next to the pancakes. Go sit by the fire, I'll bring you some!"

"Huh? Why did I make all of this for you? It's Christmas, isn't it?"

"...n-not for two more days?"

"..."

"Just think of it like a Christmas ambush, okay?"

states.title: Spring's First Blossom
states.description:
Rizette has never spent a New Year in the capital.

She's heard the stories, of course. Dancers puppeting magnificent dragons weave up and down the streets; revelers light the moonless sky with fireworks and lantern light. Families will travel from across the kingdom to spend one night at home, and everyone's made so much food, not a soul goes hungry.

To a country girl like her, it sounds too exciting to be real. But she does have a question.

What is she to wear on a night as grand as that?

states.title: Spring's First Blossom
states.description:
Rizette's hometown welcomed the New Year warmly, but with little fanfare. She remembers the fragrant bread and soup her mother would make, how she and Theo would run around the house, banishing evil spirits with brooms and feather dusters. After dinner, with bellies full and fire crackling, her father would lean back in his chair and read from their book of fairy tales. Theo always fell asleep before he finished.

Compared to the lanterns and fireworks of the capital, the New Year of her home is nothing. But she misses it all the same.

states.title: Spring's First Blossom
states.description:
"It's been such a lovely night. Lovelier than I could have imagined."

"Hmm? Oh, yes. This is my first New Year I've spent in the capital. Did something give it away?"

"Well, you can't blame me for staring at a sky like this. It's beautiful. Like the stars have come down to celebrate with us. I've never seen anything like it!"

"Nights like tonight, when everyone in the world is smiling...don't they remind you what's worth fighting for?"

"Yes. Happy New Year, friend. Let's stay beneath this sky a little longer."

states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
Long, long ago, a destitute kingdom fell under siege. Fearing his people's poverty would mean their downfall, the king commissioned a group of alchemists to craft him an endless source of gold.

After some months, the alchemists presented him with a tiny beast. In front of him, they fed it scrap metal, spoiled food, all manner of the kingdom's waste -- and he watched as horns of solid gold grew on its body.

The king wept for joy, for with this monster, he'd saved his kingdom.

Or so he thought.

states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
Months passed. The relentless war continued, and even with the gold from the beast's severed horns, the kingdom could not push the enemy back. Desperate, the king begged the alchemists to push the creature further. To feed it more and more. To curse it with hunger so great, it could never stop eating.

The alchemists warned the king that such a curse could have consequences more dire than any war, but he wouldn't listen. They'd given this monster life themselves. Surely, if it ever turned on them, they could force it back into servitude.

states.title: Adamantine Maw
states.description:
The alchemists obediently gave the creature a hunger no living thing has ever known. With every anguished day, its body shifted and grew, until it had to be chained down with its own gold, the one thing it could not consume.

In the end, the king's gambit paid off. The beast's torture earned him enough gold to win his war. When the enemy messenger arrived with terms of surrender, however, he found a sinkhole in place of a palace.

The monster could not consume its chains, it was true. But it had grown blades on its arms to break them.

states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
There was a world before the wall.

A world before black rain, and dragonfire, and rations that never last. A world before the undead, where the sky shone bright and the spring brought flowers.

She has never seen such things herself. The monstrous goddess started her war before she was ever born, leaving her with nothing but legends and tales of days gone by. Most these days don't have even that, so beaten down by the undead siege that every hope has fled them.

But not her. Never her.

Even knowing they will die, the sakuras bloom. And so will she.

states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
The war with the undead started eighteen years ago, but the history of her clan stretches much, much further. Though her village was little more than a farming settlement by the time it fell, it guarded an ancient samurai fortress. The last lord to reside in that fortress met his death at the hands of the undead. Now, like everything else, it sits abandoned.

Shiori has never set foot inside it herself. As bodyguards of the samurai, her family was not permitted within its walls without their master's blessing.

But sometimes in her dreams, she travels to its gates to stand watch.

states.title: Steel-Edged Sakura
states.description:
A thousand years ago, Shiori's ancestor swore to guard a noble samurai with his life. That vow has passed from generation to generation, and now rests with her. The man who first made it could have never imagined the world his descendant would find herself in, but even so, she holds the promise fast.

Her father may have died in battle. Her mother may have fallen when her village fell. But she understands something the goddess, in all her immortal glory, never could.

As long as she honors her family's memory, they are never truly gone.

states.title: Silk-Veiled Siren
states.description:
―― Beginning mission report.

I've just departed Hymelian territory. Duke Valenne welcomed me to his feast most warmly, and his son even more so. I fortuitously happened by his bedroom in the morning -- just in time to be caught. Your wisdom proved invaluable yet again, master. The flustered lover truly is my most convincing mask.

The rumor mill has already started churning. As you requested, the boy is unharmed, though I can't say the same for his reputation. I expect his betrothed's house to call things off within the month, which is a problem solved for us.

I remain your faithful servant. For the glory of Bergaria.

states.title: Silk-Veiled Siren
states.description:
―― Beginning mission report.

I thank you for this assignment, master. Every day I am away from Bergaria, I long to return. To work the Emperor's will in his own domain is my highest honor.

The upstart senator was bold to throw a ball while so publicly defying his regent. I've seen to his punishment. The toxin I slipped into his wine shrivels the body from the inside out. Make no mistake: everyone will know he was poisoned, and everyone will know why.

I remain your faithful servant. For the glory of Bergaria.

states.title: Silk-Veiled Siren
states.description:
―― Beginning my report.

I did it, master. The blade slid right between his ribs, just like you said it would. In the end, he didn't even scream. He just looked at me, so scared and confused, like he had no idea who could possibly want him dead.

No idea at all.

The bastard took everything from me, and he never even spared it a second thought.

(The text beyond this is illegible. The paper is wrinkled with age, and the ink is smeared with tear stains.)

states.title: Shadeborn Menace
states.description:
In a war on life itself, every death is a victory. Whether claimed in glory or claimed in the dark, it matters not; only that another soul has been brought into the fold. Few have shepherded more souls to the Goddess than the dreaded Endless Phantoms.

Some say the shadowy assassins armor themselves in the bones of their victims. Whether that's true, none can say. They move through the night indistinguishable as shadow, and all who come close enough to see the make of their armor are already good as dead.

states.title: Shadeborn Menace
states.description:
Every so often, a new undead convert catches the goddess's eye. An honored knight, a well-respected mage, a particularly clever thief; when these souls succumb to her black flame, she molds their bodies for a special purpose.

The Endless Phantoms bear no trace of their previous lives. They possess no will of their own and rarely speak. No one living knows why they were chosen for their grim task. Some believe they emerged, whole and monstrous, from the Endless Pyre itself. But they are wrong.

Orcanix's assassins were as monstrous in life as they are in undeath.

states.title: Lord of the Underworld
states.description: Having walked Erden for some time now, Lord Dis is growing fond of the place. He's considering abandoning his previous position, letting someone else manage the punishment while he relaxes seaside at Zegan Island Beach.

states.title: Gossamer Drake
states.description:
"Day 26, on the road to elven lands:

I have slept in my saddle now for three days straight. Our caravan leader insists he will not stop until he spies a baby Aethrodon -- the blasted things appear only in total safety, he says. Camping anywhere else would doom us all, he says.

Confound these elves. Pandemonium or not, we're armed to the teeth. I think we can take our chances, tiny lizard's blessing be damned."

-- From The Travels of Caerwyn

states.title: Gossamer Drake
states.description:
"Day 31, in the heart of the elven forests:

I'd seen nothing like it in all my days. Bedecked in bronze armor, brilliant as a shard of the summer sky -- this, I asked, is what comes of those skittish creatures we glimpsed on the road?

'Why else would we wait to see one before we camp?' the chieftain answered me. 'Wherever a young Aethrodon wanders, its mother guards its path. No enemy would dare approach.'"

-- From The Travels of Caerwyn

states.title: Savior of the Living City
states.description:
No one has arrived late to an inspection in six months. No one would dare. Not with the Queen of Valkyries heading the Vanguard.

As a city under constant siege, Last Bastion can't afford imperfection in its soldiers, as she so frequently reminds them. A single mistake could cost lives. Either her men rise to her standards, or they forfeit their place in her service. Time is a precious resource, and Ludmilla will waste none of it on mercy.

The goddess certainly won't.

states.title: Savior of the Living City
states.description:
She knows they call her a tyrant. In the shadows of the city she fights so hard to protect, she knows her people curse her name.

They have their reasons: hunger, cold, exhaustion. She was but a child when the invasion began, but the living complained of the very same in the world before. Perhaps some things never change. Or perhaps, no matter their circumstances, people find a way to be discontent.

It doesn't matter. Let them mutter and curse as much as they please. As long as they survive their wretched nights, she has done her duty.

states.title: Savior of the Living City
states.description:
Those who came up through the Vanguard with Ludmilla insist she wasn't always cruel. With every passing day, fewer and fewer believe them. Some have even begun to doubt it themselves.

No woman is born a tyrant, however, least of all a woman so fervently devoted to seeing her people safe. Perhaps in another world, one without the relentless undead siege, she could temper her tactical brilliance with compassion. Perhaps, where she can manage it, she still tries.

She knows well it is better to be loved than feared. But it is better still to be alive.

states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion. There is no rebellion in Last Bastion.

The Vanguard like to think it will become true if they say it enough, but they know they are kidding themselves. A year ago, the missing supplies, the vandalism, even the accidents could be waved away as the work of disorganized malcontents. Today, they are too frequent, too directed against the city's tyrannical leader. A reckoning, long overdue, will soon arrive.

She will not be silenced now. She has waited far too long.

states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
Stories of the rebel leader pervade Last Bastion's populace, whispered so as not to attract a passing patrol's attention. Spreading rumors of subversion is tantamount to treason in a city that depends so heavily on its unity, but even so, the lure of the forbidden keeps Astrid's infamy alive.

The stories say she can take on six soldiers at once, that she can move without a sound, that no one has ever seen her sleep. But for every believer who marvels at her deeds, another skeptic scoffs.

Surely if she possessed such power, she would have already won.

states.title: Voice of Defiance
states.description:
It may seem an eternity under the undead siege, but Astrid has only been officially dead for two years. Most still remember her and her father, and they remember the last night either of them was seen alive.

No one knows how the outer wall exploded. Only that it did, and that countless people burned to keep the city safe. Ludmilla attests that the commander and his daughter died in the blaze, but there are some who tell a different story.

They say that Astrid never agreed to the sacrifice of her people, and that she's come to claim their vengeance.

states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Devil. Fifteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of duplicity, corruption, and inescapable fate.

Cyrus learned to read the cards as a boy, the poor bastard of a lord. Even as a child, he was no fool; his use to his father would end the moment his wife bore him a true heir. Rather than wait to be unceremoniously murdered, he ran away and joined an underground ring of thieves masquerading as soothsayers and fortune tellers. His skill with a deck served him well, but unlike his comrades, Cyrus told no lies.

The misfortunes he predicted had a way of coming true.

states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Tower. Sixteenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of suffering, disaster, and unforeseen consequences.

It was a simple enough job. A mercenary named Rody hired him to investigate a collapsing mine. Nothing more than a tidy purse of gold should have come of it. But somehow, Cyrus found himself embroiled in a fight to save the world.

The others at Rody's side were good, honest. Not like him. They played their parts in the grand pantomime well, but he could see the Pandemonium for what it was. A scheme.

Somewhere, someone was pulling their strings, and when everything finally went wrong, he vowed to find out who.

states.title: Mercurial Mastermind
states.description:
The Star. Seventeenth Arcana of the Tarot. The card of clarity, hope, and renewed purpose.

After much searching, he found his answers. Their god was the greatest con man of all -- but he did not run his game without reason. In the divine darkness, Cyrus glimpsed the worlds beyond Erden, and all the unknowable powers that resided there. He understood then that Rody had died not to protect Erden from the Pandemonium, but to protect it from them.

He would not let his sacrifice go to waste. As his friend had before him, he would take on the mantle of a god and play a newfound role.

states.title: Lightbearing Leader
states.description:
They came in numbers Rigland couldn't hope to match. They armed themselves with technology the world had never seen. And when the time came to stand against them, the Red Dragon Knights crumbled from the inside. Not even at the height of the Pandemonium had the future seemed so bleak.

But Rizette had no choice but to move forward.

With her brother at her side, she fled the fallen capital and returned to her hometown: the ruined Westcarte, where her adventure began. Once more, in a place of desolation, hope would rise anew.

states.title: Lightbearing Leader
states.description:
Though her kingdom is conquered, its people aren't yet crushed. No matter what indignities the Bergarian soldiers force upon them, no matter the oppression they face, they know that somewhere out there, someone is fighting for their freedom. And they want to aid her however they can.

At the very whisper of Rizette's presence, crates of food and weapons appear at her door. Recruits band together, crude weapons in hand, untrained but ready to fight.

Her rebellion may be young, as fragile as a fawn finding its legs, but as long as Rigland's spirit burns bright, she knows she has nothing to fear.

states.title: Lightbearing Leader
states.description:
Rizette once thought the Cerulean Crest gave her strength. And perhaps, at the time, it did. The lonely, scared girl she was never would have believed she could be a hero without its power.

In the end, though, that power was a lie.

She isn't like Finn. She had no legacy to inherit, no Crest to forge for herself. After the Pandemonium, destiny seemed to quietly leave her behind.

No one thought the burden of the kingdom's hopes would fall on her. But fall they did, and even so, she found the strength to stand.

states.title: Gilded Heiress
states.description:
Gloria Valen, daughter of Maximilius Valen, was born under a lucky star. Her mother told her so every day of her childhood. Gloria believed it until the day she died, leaving her in the sole care of her cruel and miserly father. Somehow, her stars never seemed so lucky after that.

She never wanted for material comfort. Her father's profound wealth bought her the finest cuisine, the most elegant fashion, and invitations to every noble estate in Bergaria. For many it would have been enough, but like any true heiress, she dreamed of more.

She dreamed of a life worth more than her family name.

states.title: Gilded Heiress
states.description:
Unlike most Bergarian nobility, Gloria's father gathered his fortune not through inheritance, but through business. His eye for rich alchemical ore made him a talented magical merchant, selling enchanted weapons to the highest bidder regardless of allegiance or creed. Unsurprisingly, this earned him many enemies; Gloria grew up dodging kidnappers and assassins alike.

Lord Valen always believed his daughter's greatest worth to him would be her eventual marriage. He never saw the way she tinkered with his cast-off gems. He never saw the way she found beauty and power in what he rejected.

But one day -- maybe thanks to that lucky star of hers -- someone else did.

states.title: Spritely Spearmaster
states.description:
Lillian Rosalinde von Dreyer was born with a certain problem. Since its founding, her bloodline has been known for two things: indefatigable spirit and truly enormous size. The story goes that, after giving birth to five strapping boys, her mother swore that her sixth would be the strongest warrior of them all. So, naturally, Lillian arrived not only small for her family, but small for any Hymelian.

Only the foolish dare bring up this little legend around her, however. She tends to challenge anyone who does to a duel, to prove that her mother's prediction was in no way false.

states.title: Spritely Spearmaster
states.description:
Lillian's father worried that having five elder brothers might overwhelm a dainty little girl. However, his worry soon proved to be quite unfounded. More often than not, Lillian was the one who sent her brothers running, beating them back from her dolls with a tree branch spear. When they heard of the Hymelian princess's penchant for combat, Lillian's parents sent her to be her handmaiden -- or perhaps, more accurately, her apprentice.

 Though Maxima fights with a sword instead of a spear, Lillian has taken her aggressive philosophy quite to heart. In battle, she ensures her opponents never gain an inch on her allies, no matter what she has to do.

states.title: Blade of the Summer Sun
states.description:
The villagers called little Kisara the terror of their town.

No corner of their farming hamlet was spared her whirlwind mischief. The young girl practically vibrated with energy -- energy her traditional, rule-abiding parents couldn't handle. The more they forced her into chores and quiet reading, the more she rebelled, mouthing off to anyone who'd listen and breaking valuable tools in the name of playing 'samurai.' 

At their wits' end, her mother and father attempted to betroth her to a wealthy merchant in the city, but a wandering swordsman instead asked for the chance to take Kisara on as his apprentice. He alone saw the burning heart her misbehavior masked.

states.title: Blade of the Summer Sun
states.description:
At first, Kisara rebelled against her master's strictness. She had an undeniable talent for the sword. He'd even told her so. Why did he insist on repetitive drills, boring meals, and hours upon hours of meditation?

He replied that meditation nourished her mind, rice nourished her body, and drills nourished her blade. Should any one of these things go dull, all the talent in the world couldn't keep her alive.

Eventually, she saw the wisdom of his words, and channeled her boundless energy into a signature fighting style. Long before she left her master, tales of her unrelenting speed and strength made her enemies tremble.

states.title: Blade of the Summer Sun
states.description:
She could have had anything she desired. Wealth, power, fame -- more than one nobleman offered her these things to her, if she would swear to serve them exclusively. 

Instead, she charged them double her usual fee, then turned around and helped their villagers for nothing at all.

Kisara was no fool. She'd learned long ago that many samurai hid behind honor to do terrible things. Even she could not always be a hero. But she could make the world a gentler place the only way she knew how.

She could pass on her wisdom and ideals to an apprentice, as her master had done before her.

states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
In ancient times, Bergaria was not a vast, imposing empire, but a scattered collection of tribes. The harsh mountains left little room to farm, so famines came both fierce and frequent. If they hoped to survive, peasants would have to swear their service to a warlord, turning their eyes away from the cruelty they inflicted.

The priestesses of Chronos, hidden in a northern temple, were spared their land's turmoil. In exchange for keeping watch over time's flow, the Primordial granted them both safety and power. In all of Erden, only they could manipulate time. 

Rolotia, gifted with the most prodigious time magic seen in generations, always felt that fate was more a curse than a blessing.

states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
As a child, Rolotia drove the other priestesses to no end of frustration. Though she was never malicious, never drunk on her own power, she proved too spirited to abide by the order's rules of isolation. Her eyes forever strayed to the horizon. Had she not been such a prodigy, perhaps the elders might have let her go. But doing so would have been too great a risk.

Before long, their indecision became their undoing. One morning, when the priestesses went to wake Rolotia for prayer, they found her staff gone and her bedroom empty. She had frozen time and escaped into the world so long forbidden to her.

states.title: Sorceress of Dawn
states.description:
There was another rule that bound the priestesses of Chronos. No matter what tragedies befell mankind, they were never to reverse history once it had been decided. A single altered choice could change the future in ways no mortal could foresee.

After her escape, Rolotia cast aside her powers, aware of the temptation they would pose. But when her beloved—a general in the Bergarian army—fell, she rewrote time itself to bring him back.  The consequences of that sin would echo for a thousand years.

Even so, she greets each dawn with hope, for each new day offers her its chance of redemption.

states.title: Devoted Timekeeper
states.description:
Deep within the northernmost mountains of Bergaria, an order of secretive priestesses makes their home. Few know of their existence, and even fewer could locate them if pressed. Long ago, Chronos herself gifted their ancestors with power over time, a gift she bestowed only on them. If time magic were to spread among the peoples of Erden, history itself could dissolve into a meaningless paradox.

And so it is the priestesses' duty to watch the flow of time, ever neutral, ever detached from the world. It is a peaceful life, free of worry. Few acolytes find reason to complain.

But Gemina's curiosity was much too great to let her remain an observer.

states.title: Devoted Timekeeper
states.description:
Gemina heard legends as she was growing up. Cautionary tales of a priestesses who ran from the temple and caused untold disaster. Her elders used their tragic fates to scare the acolytes into compliance. Gemina, however, would not be swayed.

She understood the importance of her order's neutrality. Those trusted to oversee time itself had a great responsibility, greater than most in Erden would ever have to face. But how could the guardians of history perform their duty if the world was nothing to them but a collection of sad stories?

That is why she ran away. Gemina escaped the priestesses' temple not to defile her duty, but to embrace its truest purpose.

states.title: Doomsday Dragon God
states.description: The Dragon God's name is written in ancient legends. The leader of all dragons, the Bringer of the End - Bahamut. For a time, he was thought to be mere legend— the possibility that he might truly exist, a nightmare too horrible to contemplate. A single dragon can destroy a town; what would their god be capable of?

states.title: Doomsday Dragon God
states.description:
The Wyrm riseth from the abyss. He commandeth the flame of blight. His wings bring dark like unto blackest night. He heraldeth the end, not in a rebirth, but in the ruin of all that is.

—An ancient inscription

states.title: Doomsday Dragon God
states.description: At cataclysm's end, a single living being remains, an old man, his life fading fast. Through the flames and ash around him, he glimpses only the hint of a shadow,the suggestion of a titanic wing. For a moment, he sees the Dark Dragon God. But as his eyes cloud and close for the last time, he breathes a prophecy:"Someday, you, too, shall end..."

states.title: Maiden of Radiance
states.description:
Jeanne and Mikaela d'Arc were born a strange set of twins. Mikaela arrived first, a few minutes before the last midnight of winter, while Jeanne came in the first hours of spring. The town priestess insisted the birth was an omen from His Radiance, though she never said what it foretold.

Though technically the same age, it seemed only natural to call Mikaela the older sister. She was, after all, the more outgoing one, the braver one. The town cooed over her, the little warrior, so devoted to seeing Jeanne safe and happy.

And Jeanne loved Mikaela more than anyone in the world.

states.title: Maiden of Radiance
states.description:
Though Jeanne often stayed in the shadow of her sister, the village noticed her piety. She obeyed the church's teachings, prayed often, and helped who she could in her own shy way. Many suspected she would become the priestess's acolyte when she came of age, just as they said Mikaela would join the holy army.

Sadly, neither would come to pass.

When the war took a turn for the worse, their village found itself alone in the dark, without defense. Jeanne and Mikaela ran for the church together, hoping that if they reached its sacred ground, they'd be safe.

They were right: the demons couldn't breach the church. But only Jeanne made it there alive.

states.title: Maiden of Radiance
states.description:
"And to the maiden, in her grief, an angel shall appear.
Robed in white, her wings will scatter sorrow,
sowing courage where once doubt reigned.

In the falling darkness, she will be a light unto
her people, a beacon of His Radiance's blessings,
pure in thought and deed. A queen to her country,
a servant to her god.

By her hand shall her home be saved.
By her faith shall the farmer be made soldier.
By her sacrifice shall the demons break and burn.

So has His Radiance shown me, and so shall it be."

-- Prophecy of Holy Maiden Seraphine
Jeanne's twin sister and guardian angel, revived with holy power to watch over her once again.
Jeanne's twin sister and guardian angel, revived with holy power to watch over her once again.
Jeanne's twin sister and guardian angel, revived with holy power to watch over her once again.

states.title: Pious Acolyte
states.description:
It is a terrible thing to grow up in war. But while Eveline was young, the demons seemed so far away. The type of thing only soldiers worried about. The power of His Radiance and the Holy Maiden kept the rest of them safe.

Mikaela, Eveline's longtime friend, always boasted that she would join the army when she grew up. Eveline wished she could share her courage. She longed to serve her god and her kingdom, but her strength was never in swordsmanship. It was in her piety, which was rivaled only by little Jeanne, and in her wit, which was rivaled by none.

states.title: Pious Acolyte
states.description:
When the demons finally swept through their village, she tried to help Jeanne through her loss. Mikaela had given strength to them both, but as important as she'd been to Eveline, to Jeanne, she was irreplaceable. And now she was gone.

In the end, the grief turned too much to bear. Eveline pledged herself as an acolyte to the church and gradually lost touch with her old friend. But she never forgot about her, and she never lost faith that someday, light would return to her life.

That faith would be rewarded a thousand times over in the years to come.

states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
Growing up in the mountains of Bergaria, Rolotia knew only chilly springs, frost-kissed autumns, and winters so bitter they killed all memory of warmth.

Now that she's made it to the beach for the first time, she's as thrilled as she is overwhelmed. The sun shines so strongly she can feel its rays brush her skin. The sea stretches from her feet to the horizon, free and untamed, nothing like the icy peaks that shadowed her childhood.

If this is summer, she never wants it to end.

states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
Rolotia loves the beach. She'd much rather race across golden sand than harsh, glinting snow. Unfortunately, sunshine and her pale northern skin don't get along well.

After that first day, which saw her going to bed a painful shade of crimson, Rolotia has come prepared. She persuaded Rizette to part with some sun-cloaking cream in exchange for a few magically extended moments with Finn. She made herself an oasis of shade with her umbrella. But what is she to do when she wants to join her friends in the water?

Perhaps a hat can keep the sun away.

states.title: Lady of Eternal Summer
states.description:
The close of her vacation has filled Rolotia with a surprising melancholy. As the Avatar of Time, she's learned well that all good things must end. Surely a summer is nothing to mourn.

Yet each wave heralds their return to war, to worry, to reality. Could she ask Chronos to keep them here forever, suspended in a perfect summer's day, never to know suffering again?

No. The Primordial of Time would never allow it. As long as they draw breath, all living things must change. That is the foremost law of time.

But as long as she keeps the memory in her heart, Rolotia's first summer need never truly end.

states.title: Sunkissed Mirage
states.description:
People stare at Elmina no matter where she goes.

In human kingdoms, they stare because she's an elf. In the fair-skinned north, her tan draws curious attention. And when she's on the beach...well. She could do without the open-mouthed stares, but she likes bikinis best, so that's what she'll wear.

To tell the truth, being noticed takes some getting used to for a former street kid. That's why she learned to be stealthy. Keeping invisible saved her hide too many times to count, but it's no way to live. Not anymore.

She can slip into shadow whenever she wants. Why not enjoy a day in the sun?

states.title: Sunkissed Mirage
states.description:
Elmina didn't grow up with tropical heat, but she thinks it suits her. Humidity works wonders for her complexion, and the sun brings out the natural highlights in her hair. She doesn't have to worry about burns much either, though Callen still lectures her until she puts on proper protection.

Still, when the sun grows too bright even for her, she enjoys a cool retreat into the forest. Walking through the shade at the hottest time of day is a rare pleasure for one used to shivering in the cold.

And stumbling upon an unexpected waterfall? That's the kind of memory that begs to be shared with another.

states.title: Sunkissed Mirage
states.description:
Elves can hold their breath five times longer than most other races. Did you know that? Elmina finds most people don't.

It's a handy talent, one that's saved her life more than once. Not because she was much of a swimmer in her childhood, but because a little thief frequently finds herself in situations where a single audible breath could kill her.

Even these days, she feels a strange kind of peace when she isn't breathing. In those moments before her lungs start to burn, she knows she's safe.

She supposes she could stay beneath the waves, in a safe and breathless haven. But she doesn't need to anymore. Her friends have her back now, and they don't want her to be silent.

states.title: Sweet Beach Temptation
states.description: "My sweet angel, I'm heeeeere! We made it to the island at last. At last. I thought that horrible boat trip would never end. All those storms, all those people packed so close together. It's a miracle I didn't lose any of my things! (Don't worry, I checked my bags as soon as we docked. All my swimsuits are safe and sound∼) Wish you were with me, cuddlebug!" Shortly after landing at their island resort, a Hymelian noble was horrified to find her jewelry box had vanished. Alira stole it for the war effort, of course. But surely the emperor will let her keep a piece or two for herself.

states.title: Sweet Beach Temptation
states.description:
"My most handsome darling,

Ugh. How gloomy everyone on this island is! Whenever I start to relax, someone has to bring up the invasion—or worse, the rebels. As soon as I get back, you owe me a kiss for each time I had to hear 'Blue Dragon Knights.' I'm warning you, it's a lot! ♡

Wish you were with me, pumpkin!"

In the middle of the night, resort guards arrested a wealthy merchant for treason. Supposedly, his secret correspondence with the Rigland resistance had been pinned to his door with knives. Alira usually prefers a more subtle approach, but this vacation put her in a more theatrical mood than usual.

states.title: Sweet Beach Temptation
states.description:
The emperor ordered her to send a coded letter each day she was away. Alira has faithfully carried out her duties as his spy, sowing fear of the Empire among Erden's most wealthy and elite. But there are some secrets she must keep even from her master.

Who knows why Fate would bring the man who killed her family here now? Who knows why She would make him untouchable, far too crucial to Bergaria's war to die at her hands? Who knows what cruel joke the world will play on her next?

She'll let the bastard live. For now. But he owes her a debt in blood, and one day, Alira will see it paid.

states.title: Fiery Island Flower
states.description: She's traveled from the great Mount Fornax to the sprawling ports of Zuthua. She's conquered the art of monster raising, learned fire magic, and even made a friend or two along the way. But now, Antares faces her most daunting challenge yet. Before the summer ends, she must learn to relax! Luna told her so the last time they met. Of course, she meant it as a joke, but never let it be said that Antares disappoints her idols. She stayed up all night researching the ideal vacation, and by the next morning, she was off to Laomis, a freshly sewn swimsuit in her bag.

states.title: Fiery Island Flower
states.description:
Sitting by the pool with flowers in her hair and a picnic basket at her side, Antares may seem like a natural at this 'vacation' thing. But don't be fooled. Relaxing is harder than it looks.
